3	from typing import Optional, Callable, TypeAlias, Tuple
4	from dataclasses import dataclass
5	import enum
6	
7	import cutlass
8	import cutlass.cute as cute
9	from cutlass import Float32, Int32, Uint32, const_expr
10	from cutlass.cutlass_dsl import min as dsl_min
11	
12	from quack import layout_utils
13	import flash_attn.cute.utils as utils
14	from flash_attn.cute.block_info import BlockInfo
15	from flash_attn.cute.seqlen_info import SeqlenInfoQK
16	from flash_attn.cute.utils import AuxData
17	
18	MaskGenFn: TypeAlias = Callable[[int], Uint32]
19	MASK_R2P_CHUNK_SIZE: int = 32
20	
21	
22	@cute.jit
23	def call_mask_mod(
24	    mask_mod: cutlass.Constexpr,
25	    batch_idx,
26	    head_idx,
27	    q_idx,
28	    kv_idx,
29	    seqlen_info,
30	    aux_data: AuxData,
31	):
32	    # Compatibility shim for pre-aux_scalars mask_mod callables.
33	    if const_expr(aux_data.scalars is not None):
34	        return mask_mod(
35	            batch_idx,
36	            head_idx,
37	            q_idx,
38	            kv_idx,
39	            seqlen_info,
40	            aux_data.tensors,
41	            aux_data.scalars,
42	        )
43	    return mask_mod(
44	        batch_idx,
45	        head_idx,
46	        q_idx,
47	        kv_idx,
48	        seqlen_info,
49	        aux_data.tensors,
50	    )
51	
52	
53	@cute.jit
54	def r2p_bitmask_below(limit: Int32, s: int) -> Uint32:
55	    """32-bit R2P bitmask keeping positions < limit (exclusive upper bound).
56	
57	    Positions 0..limit-1 in chunk `s` get bit=1 (keep), the rest bit=0 (mask).
58	    Uses inline PTX to avoid shift-by-type-width UB.
59	    """
60	    m = max((s + 1) * MASK_R2P_CHUNK_SIZE - limit, 0)
61	    return utils.shr_u32(Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), Uint32(m))
62	
63	
64	@cute.jit
65	def r2p_bitmask_above(limit: Int32, s: int) -> Uint32:
66	    """32-bit R2P bitmask keeping positions >= limit (inclusive lower bound).
67	
68	    Positions limit..31 in chunk `s` get bit=1 (keep), the rest bit=0 (mask).
69	    Uses inline PTX to avoid shift-by-type-width UB.
70	    """
71	    n = max(limit - s * MASK_R2P_CHUNK_SIZE, 0)
72	    return utils.shl_u32(Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), Uint32(n))
73	
74	
75	@cute.jit
76	def mask_r2p_lambda(
77	    X: cute.Tensor,
78	    mask_gen_fn: cutlass.Constexpr[MaskGenFn],
79	    rank1: bool = False,
80	) -> None:
81	    """Apply R2P masking with a custom bitmask generator.
82	
83	    mask_gen_fn(chunk_idx: constexpr int) -> Uint32:
84	        Returns a 32-bit bitmask for the chunk. Bit i set means column
85	        chunk_idx * chunk_size + i is KEPT; bit i clear means masked to -inf.
86	    """
87	    ncol = const_expr(cute.size(X.shape[cute.rank(X) - 1]) if not rank1 else cute.size(X.shape))
88	    # 32-column chunks. The mask_gen_fn returns a Uint32 bitmask (1=keep).
89	    CHUNK_SIZE = MASK_R2P_CHUNK_SIZE
90	    for s in cutlass.range_constexpr(cute.ceil_div(ncol, CHUNK_SIZE)):
91	        mask = mask_gen_fn(s)
92	        # This needs to be range_constexpr, o/w the compiler can't generate the R2P instruction
93	        for i in cutlass.range_constexpr(min(CHUNK_SIZE, ncol - s * CHUNK_SIZE)):
94	            in_bound = cutlass.Boolean(mask & (Uint32(1) << i))
95	            c = s * CHUNK_SIZE + i
96	            if const_expr(rank1):
97	                X[c] = X[c] if in_bound else -Float32.inf
98	            else:
99	                for r in cutlass.range_constexpr(cute.size(X.shape[0])):
100	                    X[r, c] = X[r, c] if in_bound else -Float32.inf
101	
102	
103	@cute.jit
104	def sm90_col_to_r2p_idx(col_limit: Int32) -> Int32:
105	    """Transform SM90 MMA column coordinate to R2P element index.
106	
107	    SM90 MMA accumulator column indices are non-contiguous: 0, 1, 8, 9, 16, 17, ...
108	    Element indices are contiguous: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, ...
109	    This converts a column-space threshold to element-space for r2p_bitmask_below/above.
110	    """
111	    return col_limit // 8 * 2 + min(col_limit % 8, 2)
112	
113	
114	@cute.jit
115	def row_to_r2p_idx(x: Int32, num_rep: int, num_wg: int) -> Int32:
116	    """Convert a row coordinate to an R2P element index in the warp-group interleaved layout.
117	
118	    In the SM100 backward pass, 2 warp groups share TMEM. The TMEM load atom
119	    distributes rows in an interleaved pattern: elements 0..num_rep-1 map to
120	    rows 0..num_rep-1 (warp group 0), elements num_rep..2*num_rep-1 map to
121	    rows num_rep*num_wg..num_rep*num_wg+num_rep-1 (warp group 1), and so on.
122	    Row-coordinate thresholds (causal limits, window bounds, uih_len) must be
123	    converted to element indices before use with r2p_bitmask_above/below.
124	
125	    Rows not owned by this thread (in the gap between warp groups) are clamped
126	    to the boundary element index, which is safe because R2P thresholds are
127	    monotonic.
128	
129	    Example with num_rep=16, num_wg=2:
130	        row  0 -> elem  0,  row 15 -> elem 15,
131	        row 16 -> elem 16 (clamped), row 31 -> elem 16 (clamped),
132	        row 32 -> elem 16, row 33 -> elem 17, row 47 -> elem 31.
133	    """
134	    return x // (num_rep * num_wg) * num_rep + min(x % (num_rep * num_wg), num_rep)
135	
136	
137	@cute.jit
138	def apply_packed_mask_chunk(
139	    X: cute.Tensor,
140	    chunk_idx: cutlass.Constexpr[int],
141	    mask: Uint32,
142	) -> None:
143	    """Apply one 32-bit keep mask to one 32-column chunk.
144	
145	    The one-iteration chunk loop keeps the same lowering pattern as mask_r2p_lambda.
146	    """
147	    ncol = const_expr(cute.size(X.shape))
148	    col_base = chunk_idx * MASK_R2P_CHUNK_SIZE
149	    for s in cutlass.range_constexpr(1):
150	        for i in cutlass.range_constexpr(
151	            min(MASK_R2P_CHUNK_SIZE, ncol - col_base - s * MASK_R2P_CHUNK_SIZE)
152	        ):
153	            in_bound = cutlass.Boolean(mask & (Uint32(1) << i))
154	            c = col_base + s * MASK_R2P_CHUNK_SIZE + i
155	            X[c] = X[c] if in_bound else -Float32.inf
156	
157	
158	@dataclass(frozen=True)
159	class AttentionMask:
160	    tile_m: cutlass.Constexpr[int]
161	    tile_n: cutlass.Constexpr[int]
162	    seqlen_info: SeqlenInfoQK
163	    window_size_left: Optional[Int32] = None
164	    window_size_right: Optional[Int32] = None
165	    qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa: cutlass.Constexpr[int] = 1  # only pass in if we're doing PackGQA
166	    swap_AB: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False
167	
168	    @property
169	    def seqlen_q(self) -> Int32:
170	        return self.seqlen_info.seqlen_q
171	
172	    @property
173	    def seqlen_k(self) -> Int32:
174	        return self.seqlen_info.seqlen_k
175	
176	    @cute.jit
177	    def apply_mask(
178	        self,
179	        acc_S: cute.Tensor,
180	        batch_idx: cutlass.Int32,
181	        head_idx: cutlass.Int32,
182	        m_block: cutlass.Int32,
183	        n_block: cutlass.Int32,
184	        thr_mma: cute.TiledMma,
185	        mask_seqlen: cutlass.Constexpr[bool],
186	        mask_causal: cutlass.Constexpr[bool],
187	        mask_local: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
188	        mask_mod: cutlass.Constexpr[Optional[Callable]] = None,
189	        aux_data: AuxData = AuxData(),
190	        fastdiv_mods=(None, None),
191	    ) -> None:
192	        assert not (mask_causal and mask_local), "mask_causal and mask_local cannot be both True"
193	        acc_S_mn = layout_utils.reshape_acc_to_mn(acc_S, transpose=self.swap_AB)
194	        acc_shape = (self.tile_m, self.tile_n)
195	        cS = cute.make_identity_tensor(acc_shape if not self.swap_AB else acc_shape[::-1])
196	        tScS_mn = layout_utils.reshape_acc_to_mn(thr_mma.partition_C(cS), transpose=self.swap_AB)
197	        # We use t0ScS as these indices are known at compile time. We then must subtract the
198	        # column limit by the thread column offset.
199	        t0ScS_mn = layout_utils.reshape_acc_to_mn(
200	            thr_mma.get_slice(0).partition_C(cS), transpose=self.swap_AB
201	        )
202	        ROW = 0 if const_expr(not self.swap_AB) else 1
203	        COL = 1 if const_expr(not self.swap_AB) else 0
204	        thr_col_offset = tScS_mn[0][COL]
205	        # To handle edge cases of completely masked out rows where n_block_max = 0,
206	        # we treat negative n_blocks as 0th n_block
207	        # TODO: find more transparent solution
208	        if n_block < 0:
209	            n_block = 0
210	        seqlenk_col_limit = self.seqlen_k - n_block * self.tile_n - thr_col_offset
211	        if const_expr(not mask_causal and not mask_local and mask_mod is None):
212	            if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
213	                r2p = const_expr(not self.swap_AB)
214	                if const_expr(not r2p):
215	                    # traverse column index.
216	                    for c in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[1]), unroll_full=True):
217	                        oob = t0ScS_mn[0, c][COL] >= seqlenk_col_limit
218	                        for r in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[0]), unroll_full=True):
219	                            acc_S_mn[r, c] = -Float32.inf if oob else acc_S_mn[r, c]
220	                else:
221	                    seqlenk_col_limit_r2p = sm90_col_to_r2p_idx(seqlenk_col_limit)
222	                    mask_r2p_lambda(acc_S_mn, lambda s: r2p_bitmask_below(seqlenk_col_limit_r2p, s))
223	
224	        elif const_expr(
225	            not mask_causal and not mask_local and mask_mod is not None
226	        ):  # FlexAttention mask mod
227	            nrow = const_expr(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[0]))
228	            ncol = const_expr(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[1]))
229	            has_fastdiv = const_expr(
230	                fastdiv_mods is not None
231	                and fastdiv_mods[0] is not None
232	                and fastdiv_mods[1] is not None
233	            )
234	            wrap_aux_indices = const_expr(
235	                has_fastdiv and mask_seqlen and const_expr(aux_data.tensors is not None)
236	            )
237	
238	            for r in cutlass.range_constexpr(nrow):
239	                # Respect swap_AB: ROW/COL determine which coordinate component corresponds to Q/KV.
240	                local_row = tScS_mn[r, 0][ROW]
241	                global_row_idx = local_row + m_block * self.tile_m
242	                row_for_mod = global_row_idx
243	                head_idx_for_mod = head_idx
244	                if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa != 1):
245	                    head_offset = global_row_idx % self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa
246	                    head_idx_for_mod = head_idx * self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa + head_offset
247	                    row_for_mod = global_row_idx // self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa
248	                row_for_seqlen = row_for_mod
249	                if const_expr(wrap_aux_indices):
250	                    _, row_for_mod = divmod(row_for_mod, fastdiv_mods[0])
251	
252	                for col in cutlass.range_constexpr(ncol):
253	                    col_idx_local = t0ScS_mn[0, col][COL]
254	                    # Convert to absolute column index
255	                    global_col_idx = thr_col_offset + col_idx_local + n_block * self.tile_n
256	                    col_for_mod = global_col_idx
257	                    if const_expr(wrap_aux_indices):
258	                        _, col_for_mod = divmod(global_col_idx, fastdiv_mods[1])
259	
260	                    batch_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(batch_idx, cutlass.Int32)
261	                    head_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(head_idx_for_mod, cutlass.Int32)
262	                    q_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(row_for_mod, cutlass.Int32)
263	                    kv_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(col_for_mod, cutlass.Int32)
264	                    mask_value = call_mask_mod(
265	                        mask_mod,
266	                        batch_idx_ssa,
267	                        head_idx_ssa,
268	                        q_idx_ssa,
269	                        kv_idx_ssa,
270	                        self.seqlen_info,
271	                        aux_data,
272	                    )
273	                    cond = cutlass.Boolean(utils.ssa_to_scalar(mask_value))
274	                    if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
275	                        out_of_bounds = (row_for_seqlen >= self.seqlen_q) or (
276	                            global_col_idx >= self.seqlen_k
277	                        )
278	                        if out_of_bounds:
279	                            acc_S_mn[r, col] = -cutlass.Float32.inf
280	                        else:
281	                            acc_S_mn[r, col] = acc_S_mn[r, col] if cond else -cutlass.Float32.inf
282	                    else:
283	                        acc_S_mn[r, col] = acc_S_mn[r, col] if cond else -cutlass.Float32.inf
284	
285	        else:  # Causal or local
286	            if const_expr(not self.swap_AB):
287	                # If PackGQA, we split the work of compute divmod among threads in the same row
288	                threads_per_row = thr_mma.tv_layout_C.shape[0][0]
289	                mma_m_idx = None
290	                if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa != 1):
291	                    assert not self.swap_AB, "swap_AB with PackGQA not supported yet"
292	                    assert cute.arch.WARP_SIZE % threads_per_row == 0, (
293	                        "threads_per_row must divide WARP_SIZE"
294	                    )
295	                    assert cute.size(acc_S_mn.shape[0]) <= threads_per_row
296	                    tidx = thr_mma.thr_idx
297	                    mma_m_idx = (
298	                        m_block * self.tile_m + tScS_mn[tidx % threads_per_row, 0][0]
299	                    ) // self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa
300	                causal_row_offset = (
301	                    1 + self.seqlen_k - n_block * self.tile_n - self.seqlen_q - thr_col_offset
302	                )
303	                if const_expr(mask_causal):
304	                    r2p = const_expr(not self.swap_AB)  # R2P trick, see apply_mask_sm100
305	                    for r in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[0]), unroll_full=True):
306	                        # get the column index limit based on current row. Only consider the row index, so the column index sets to 0.
307	                        if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa == 1):
308	                            row_idx = tScS_mn[r, 0][0] + m_block * self.tile_m
309	                        else:
310	                            row_idx = utils.shuffle_sync(
311	                                mma_m_idx, r % threads_per_row, width=threads_per_row
312	                            )
313	                        col_limit_right = row_idx + causal_row_offset
314	                        if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
315	                            col_limit_right = cutlass.min(col_limit_right, seqlenk_col_limit)
316	                        if const_expr(not r2p):
317	                            # traverse column index.
318	                            for c in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[1]), unroll_full=True):
319	                                acc_S_mn[r, c] = (
320	                                    -Float32.inf
321	                                    if t0ScS_mn[0, c][1] >= col_limit_right
322	                                    else acc_S_mn[r, c]
323	                                )
324	                        else:
325	                            col_limit_r2p = sm90_col_to_r2p_idx(col_limit_right)
326	                            mask_r2p_lambda(
327	                                acc_S_mn[r, None],
328	                                lambda s: r2p_bitmask_below(col_limit_r2p, s),
329	                                rank1=True,
330	                            )
331	                else:  # Local
332	                    local_row_offset_right = (
333	                        causal_row_offset + self.window_size_right
334	                        if const_expr(self.window_size_right is not None)
335	                        else None
336	                    )
337	                    local_row_offset_left = (
338	                        causal_row_offset - 1 - self.window_size_left
339	                        if const_expr(self.window_size_left is not None)
340	                        else None
341	                    )
342	                    r2p_local = const_expr(not self.swap_AB)
343	                    for r in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[0]), unroll_full=True):
344	                        if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa == 1):
345	                            row_idx = tScS_mn[r, 0][0] + m_block * self.tile_m
346	                        else:
347	                            row_idx = utils.shuffle_sync(
348	                                mma_m_idx, r % threads_per_row, width=threads_per_row
349	                            )
350	                        if const_expr(self.window_size_right is not None):
351	                            col_limit_right = row_idx + local_row_offset_right
352	                        else:
353	                            col_limit_right = self.tile_n
354	                        if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
355	                            col_limit_right = cutlass.min(col_limit_right, seqlenk_col_limit)
356	                        col_limit_left = (
357	                            row_idx + local_row_offset_left
358	                            if const_expr(self.window_size_left is not None)
359	                            else 0
360	                        )
361	                        if const_expr(not r2p_local):
362	                            # traverse column index.
363	                            for c in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[1]), unroll_full=True):
364	                                col_idx = t0ScS_mn[0, c][1]
365	                                if col_idx >= col_limit_right or col_idx < col_limit_left:
366	                                    acc_S_mn[r, c] = -Float32.inf
367	                        else:
368	                            col_limit_right_r2p = sm90_col_to_r2p_idx(col_limit_right)
369	                            col_limit_left_r2p = sm90_col_to_r2p_idx(col_limit_left)
370	
371	                            def mask_gen_fn(s: int) -> Uint32:
372	                                return r2p_bitmask_below(
373	                                    col_limit_right_r2p, s
374	                                ) & r2p_bitmask_above(col_limit_left_r2p, s)
375	
376	                            mask_r2p_lambda(acc_S_mn[r, None], mask_gen_fn, rank1=True)
377	            else:  # swap_AB
378	                assert self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa == 1
379	                thr_row_offset = tScS_mn[0][ROW]
380	                causal_row_offset = (
381	                    seqlenk_col_limit - self.seqlen_q + m_block * self.tile_m + thr_row_offset
382	                )
383	                if const_expr(mask_causal):
384	                    for c in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[1]), unroll_full=True):
385	                        col0 = t0ScS_mn[0, c][COL]
386	                        # If col0 is beyond the column limit, we want to mask out the entire
387	                        # column, by setting row limit to be self.tile_m.
388	                        row_limit_top = (
389	                            self.tile_m
390	                            if col0 >= seqlenk_col_limit and mask_seqlen
391	                            else col0 - causal_row_offset
392	                        )
393	                        for r in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[0]), unroll_full=True):
394	                            acc_S_mn[r, c] = (
395	                                -Float32.inf
396	                                if t0ScS_mn[r, 0][ROW] < row_limit_top
397	                                else acc_S_mn[r, c]
398	                            )
399	                else:
400	                    for c in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[1]), unroll_full=True):
401	                        col0 = t0ScS_mn[0, c][COL]
402	                        # If col0 is beyond the column limit, we want to mask out the entire
403	                        # column, by setting row limit to be self.tile_m.
404	                        row_limit_top = (
405	                            self.tile_m
406	                            if col0 >= seqlenk_col_limit and mask_seqlen
407	                            else (
408	                                col0 - causal_row_offset - self.window_size_right
409	                                if const_expr(self.window_size_right is not None)
410	                                else 0
411	                            )
412	                        )
413	                        row_limit_bot = (
414	                            col0 - causal_row_offset + self.window_size_left
415	                            if const_expr(self.window_size_left is not None)
416	                            else self.tile_m
417	                        )
418	                        for r in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_mn.shape[0]), unroll_full=True):
419	                            row_idx = t0ScS_mn[r, 0][ROW]
420	                            acc_S_mn[r, c] = (
421	                                -Float32.inf
422	                                if row_idx < row_limit_top or row_idx > row_limit_bot
423	                                else acc_S_mn[r, c]
424	                            )
425	
426	    @cute.jit
427	    def apply_mask_mod_sm100_scalar(
428	        self,
429	        acc_S: cute.Tensor,
430	        tScS_t2r: cute.Tensor,
431	        m_block: Int32,
432	        n_block: Int32,
433	        mask_seqlen: cutlass.Constexpr[bool],
434	        mask_mod: cutlass.Constexpr[Callable],
435	        batch_idx: Int32,
436	        head_idx: Int32,
437	        aux_data: AuxData = AuxData(),
438	        fastdiv_mods=(None, None),
439	        head_divmod=None,
440	        check_q_boundary: bool = False,
441	    ) -> None:
442	        """Apply a scalar FlexAttention mask_mod to an SM100 accumulator fragment.
443	
444	        Each accumulator lane calls mask_mod once with logical (batch, head, q, kv)
445	        indices. Pack-GQA rows are converted back to logical q/head indices before
446	        the call. When aux tensors are present, indices are wrapped with fastdiv so
447	        mask_mod never reads outside the per-example auxiliary storage.
448	        """
449	        has_fastdiv = const_expr(
450	            fastdiv_mods is not None and fastdiv_mods[0] is not None and fastdiv_mods[1] is not None
451	        )
452	        batch_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(batch_idx, cutlass.Int32)
453	        ncol = const_expr(cute.size(tScS_t2r.shape))
454	
455	        for i in cutlass.range_constexpr(ncol):
456	            row_coord = tScS_t2r[i][0] if not self.swap_AB else tScS_t2r[i][1]
457	            col_coord = tScS_t2r[i][1] if not self.swap_AB else tScS_t2r[i][0]
458	            global_row = row_coord + m_block * self.tile_m
459	            global_col = col_coord + n_block * self.tile_n
460	
461	            if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa != 1):
462	                assert head_divmod is not None
463	                mask_row, head_offset = divmod(global_row, head_divmod)
464	                head_idx_for_mod = head_idx * self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa + head_offset
465	            else:
466	                head_idx_for_mod = head_idx
467	                mask_row = global_row
468	
469	            mask_row_for_mod = mask_row
470	            if const_expr(has_fastdiv and aux_data.tensors is not None):
471	                if check_q_boundary:
472	                    _, mask_row_for_mod = divmod(mask_row, fastdiv_mods[0])
473	            global_col_for_mod = global_col
474	            if const_expr(has_fastdiv and mask_seqlen and aux_data.tensors is not None):
475	                _, global_col_for_mod = divmod(global_col, fastdiv_mods[1])
476	
477	            head_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(head_idx_for_mod, cutlass.Int32)
478	            mask_row_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(mask_row_for_mod, cutlass.Int32)
479	            kv_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(global_col_for_mod, cutlass.Int32)
480	            mask_value = call_mask_mod(
481	                mask_mod,
482	                batch_idx_ssa,
483	                head_idx_ssa,
484	                mask_row_ssa,
485	                kv_idx_ssa,
486	                self.seqlen_info,
487	                aux_data,
488	            )
489	            cond = cutlass.Boolean(utils.ssa_to_scalar(mask_value))
490	            acc_S[i] = acc_S[i] if cond else -Float32.inf
491	            if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
492	                acc_S[i] = -Float32.inf if global_col >= self.seqlen_k else acc_S[i]
493	            if check_q_boundary:
494	                acc_S[i] = -Float32.inf if mask_row >= self.seqlen_q else acc_S[i]
495	
496	    @cute.jit
497	    def apply_mask_mod_sm100_vector(
498	        self,
499	        acc_S: cute.Tensor,
500	        tScS_t2r: cute.Tensor,
501	        m_block: Int32,
502	        n_block: Int32,
503	        mask_seqlen: cutlass.Constexpr[bool],
504	        mask_mod: cutlass.Constexpr[Callable],
505	        batch_idx: Int32,
506	        head_idx: Int32,
507	        vec_size: cutlass.Constexpr[int],
508	        aux_data: AuxData = AuxData(),
509	        fastdiv_mods=(None, None),
510	        head_divmod=None,
511	        check_q_boundary: bool = False,
512	    ) -> None:
513	        """Apply a vectorized FlexAttention mask_mod to an SM100 fragment.
514	
515	        mask_mod receives vec_size adjacent KV indices for one logical q row and
516	        returns bit-packed Uint32 keep masks. Low bits correspond to lower KV
517	        indices. The packed masks are combined with sequence-boundary checks, then
518	        applied in 32-column chunks so the final masking lowers to R2P.
519	        """
520	        has_fastdiv = const_expr(
521	            fastdiv_mods is not None and fastdiv_mods[0] is not None and fastdiv_mods[1] is not None
522	        )
523	        batch_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(batch_idx, cutlass.Int32)
524	        ncol = const_expr(cute.size(tScS_t2r.shape))
525	        mask_vals_per_apply = const_expr(max(1, vec_size // 32))
526	        calls_per_apply = const_expr(max(1, 32 // vec_size))
527	        n_calls = const_expr(cute.ceil_div(ncol, vec_size))
528	        mask_vals = cute.make_rmem_tensor(mask_vals_per_apply, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
529	
530	        # Accumulate enough vector mask_mod calls to produce 32-bit chunks that
531	        # apply_packed_mask_chunk can lower to R2P.
532	        for s in cutlass.range_constexpr(n_calls):
533	            if const_expr(s % calls_per_apply == 0):
534	                for c in cutlass.range_constexpr(mask_vals_per_apply):
535	                    mask_vals[c] = cutlass.Uint32(0)
536	            i = s * vec_size
537	            row_coord = tScS_t2r[i][0] if not self.swap_AB else tScS_t2r[i][1]
538	            col_coord = tScS_t2r[i][1] if not self.swap_AB else tScS_t2r[i][0]
539	            global_row = row_coord + m_block * self.tile_m
540	            global_col = col_coord + n_block * self.tile_n
541	            if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa != 1):
542	                assert head_divmod is not None
543	                mask_row, head_offset = divmod(global_row, head_divmod)
544	                head_idx_for_mod = head_idx * self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa + head_offset
545	            else:
546	                head_idx_for_mod = head_idx
547	                mask_row = global_row
548	            mask_row_for_mod = mask_row
549	            if const_expr(has_fastdiv and aux_data.tensors is not None):
550	                if check_q_boundary:
551	                    _, mask_row_for_mod = divmod(mask_row, fastdiv_mods[0])
552	
553	            head_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(head_idx_for_mod, cutlass.Int32).broadcast_to(
554	                (vec_size,)
555	            )
556	            mask_row_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(mask_row_for_mod, cutlass.Int32).broadcast_to(
557	                (vec_size,)
558	            )
559	            batch_idx_ssa_call = batch_idx_ssa.broadcast_to((vec_size,))
560	            kv_idx_vec = cute.make_rmem_tensor(vec_size, cutlass.Int32)
561	
562	            # Build the per-lane KV indices for this vectorized mask_mod call.
563	            for j in cutlass.range_constexpr(min(vec_size, ncol - i)):
564	                col_j_coord = tScS_t2r[i + j][1] if not self.swap_AB else tScS_t2r[i + j][0]
565	                col_j_global = col_j_coord + n_block * self.tile_n
566	                col_j_for_mod = col_j_global
567	                if const_expr(has_fastdiv and mask_seqlen and aux_data.tensors is not None):
568	                    _, col_j_for_mod = divmod(col_j_global, fastdiv_mods[1])
569	                kv_idx_vec[j] = col_j_for_mod
570	            kv_idx_ssa = kv_idx_vec.load()
571	
572	            # mask_value is already bit-packed by the vectorized mask_mod.
573	            mask_value = call_mask_mod(
574	                mask_mod,
575	                batch_idx_ssa_call,
576	                head_idx_ssa,
577	                mask_row_ssa,
578	                kv_idx_ssa,
579	                self.seqlen_info,
580	                aux_data,
581	            )
582	
583	            # For vec_size < 32, multiple mask_mod calls fill one R2P chunk.
584	            bit_offset = const_expr((s % calls_per_apply) * vec_size)
585	            seqlen_thresh_call = (
586	                self.seqlen_k - global_col if const_expr(mask_seqlen) else cutlass.Int32(0)
587	            )
588	            q_in_bounds = mask_row < self.seqlen_q if check_q_boundary else cutlass.Boolean(True)
589	            for c in cutlass.range_constexpr(mask_vals_per_apply):
590	                mask_val = mask_value[c]
591	                if const_expr(vec_size < 32):
592	                    lane_keep = utils.shr_u32(
593	                        cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF),
594	                        cutlass.Uint32(32 - vec_size),
595	                    )
596	                    mask_val = mask_val & lane_keep
597	                if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
598	                    mask_val = mask_val & r2p_bitmask_below(seqlen_thresh_call, c)
599	                if check_q_boundary:
600	                    mask_val = mask_val if q_in_bounds else cutlass.Uint32(0)
601	                mask_vals[c] = mask_vals[c] | (mask_val << bit_offset)
602	
603	            # Apply only when the 32-bit chunk is complete, or at the tile tail.
604	            is_last_in_apply = const_expr(s % calls_per_apply == calls_per_apply - 1)
605	            is_last_overall = const_expr(s == n_calls - 1)
606	            if const_expr(is_last_in_apply or is_last_overall):
607	                apply_idx = s // calls_per_apply
608	                for c in cutlass.range_constexpr(mask_vals_per_apply):
609	                    chunk_idx = apply_idx * mask_vals_per_apply + c
610	                    # Skip packed chunks that start past the accumulator fragment.
611	                    if const_expr(chunk_idx * 32 < ncol):
612	                        apply_packed_mask_chunk(acc_S, chunk_idx, mask_vals[c])
613	
614	    @cute.jit
615	    def apply_mask_sm100(
616	        self,
617	        acc_S: cute.Tensor,
618	        m_block: Int32,
619	        n_block: Int32,
620	        thr_mma: cute.TiledMma,
621	        thr_tmem_load: cute.TiledCopy,
622	        mask_seqlen: cutlass.Constexpr[bool],
623	        mask_causal: cutlass.Constexpr[bool],
624	        mask_local: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
625	        mask_mod: cutlass.Constexpr[Optional[Callable]] = None,
626	        batch_idx: Int32 = None,
627	        head_idx: Int32 = None,
628	        aux_data: AuxData = AuxData(),
629	        fastdiv_mods=(None, None),
630	        head_divmod=None,
631	        vec_size: cutlass.Constexpr[int] = 1,
632	        check_q_boundary: bool = False,
633	        r2p: bool = True,
634	        rBitmask: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
635	    ) -> None:
636	        assert not (mask_causal and mask_local), "mask_causal and mask_local cannot be both True"
637	        acc_shape = (self.tile_m, self.tile_n)
638	        cS = cute.make_identity_tensor(acc_shape if not self.swap_AB else acc_shape[::-1])
639	        tScS = thr_mma.partition_C(cS)
640	        tScS = tScS[(None, None), 0, 0]
641	        tScS_t2r = thr_tmem_load.partition_D(tScS)
642	        # To handle edge cases of completely masked out rows where n_block_max = 0,
643	        # we treat negative n_blocks as 0th n_block
644	        # TODO: find more transparent solution
645	        if n_block < 0:
646	            n_block = 0
647	        seqlenk_col_limit = self.seqlen_k - n_block * self.tile_n
648	
649	        if const_expr(rBitmask is not None):
650	            ncol_packed = const_expr(cute.size(rBitmask.shape[0]))
651	            for i in cutlass.range_constexpr(ncol_packed):
652	                col_start = 32 * i  # mask is bit-packed into uint32
653	                curr_mask_val = rBitmask[i]
654	                for j in cutlass.range_constexpr(32):
655	                    curr_col = col_start + j
656	                    mask = (curr_mask_val >> j) & 1
657	                    acc_S[curr_col] = acc_S[curr_col] if cutlass.Boolean(mask) else -Float32.inf
658	
659	        elif const_expr(not mask_causal and not mask_local and mask_mod is None):
660	            if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
661	                if const_expr(not r2p):
662	                    for i in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_t2r.shape), unroll_full=True):
663	                        # if tScS_t2r[i][1] >= seqlenk_col_limit:
664	                        #     acc_S[i] = -Float32.inf
665	                        # For some reason the 2 lines above generate really bad SASS
666	                        acc_S[i] = -Float32.inf if tScS_t2r[i][1] >= seqlenk_col_limit else acc_S[i]
667	                else:
668	                    mask_r2p_lambda(
669	                        acc_S,
670	                        lambda s: r2p_bitmask_below(seqlenk_col_limit, s),
671	                        rank1=True,
672	                    )
673	
674	        elif const_expr(not mask_causal and not mask_local and mask_mod is not None):
675	            # FlexAttention mask_mod vectorization is gated on `mask_mod.__vec_size__`.
676	            # vec_size == 1 returns a scalar Boolean. vec_size > 1 returns packed
677	            # Uint32 mask fragments: one word per 32 evaluated columns.
678	            assert vec_size % 32 == 0 or 32 % vec_size == 0, (
679	                "vec_size must divide 32 or be a multiple of 32"
680	            )
681	            if const_expr(vec_size == 1):
682	                self.apply_mask_mod_sm100_scalar(
683	                    acc_S,
684	                    tScS_t2r,
685	                    m_block,
686	                    n_block,
687	                    mask_seqlen,
688	                    mask_mod,
689	                    batch_idx,
690	                    head_idx,
691	                    aux_data,
692	                    fastdiv_mods,
693	                    head_divmod,
694	                    check_q_boundary,
695	                )
696	            else:
697	                self.apply_mask_mod_sm100_vector(
698	                    acc_S,
699	                    tScS_t2r,
700	                    m_block,
701	                    n_block,
702	                    mask_seqlen,
703	                    mask_mod,
704	                    batch_idx,
705	                    head_idx,
706	                    vec_size,
707	                    aux_data,
708	                    fastdiv_mods,
709	                    head_divmod,
710	                    check_q_boundary,
711	                )
712	
713	        else:  # Causal or local
714	            causal_row_offset = self.seqlen_k - n_block * self.tile_n - self.seqlen_q
715	            row_idx = tScS_t2r[0][0] + m_block * self.tile_m
716	            if const_expr(self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa != 1):
717	                row_idx = row_idx // self.qhead_per_kvhead_packgqa
718	            if const_expr(mask_causal):
719	                col_limit_right = row_idx + causal_row_offset + 1
720	                if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
721	                    col_limit_right = cutlass.min(col_limit_right, seqlenk_col_limit)
722	                # if cute.arch.thread_idx()[0] % 32 == 0:
723	                #     cute.printf("tidx = %d, tidx tmem = %d, row_idx = %d, col_limit_right = %d, causal_row_offset = %d\n", cute.arch.thread_idx()[0], thr_tmem_load.thr_idx, row_idx, col_limit_right, causal_row_offset)
724	                ncol = const_expr(cute.size(tScS_t2r.shape))
725	                if const_expr(not r2p):
726	                    for i in cutlass.range(ncol, unroll_full=True):
727	                        acc_S[i] = -Float32.inf if tScS_t2r[i][1] >= col_limit_right else acc_S[i]
728	                else:
729	                    mask_r2p_lambda(
730	                        acc_S,
731	                        lambda s: r2p_bitmask_below(col_limit_right, s),
732	                        rank1=True,
733	                    )
734	            else:
735	                local_row_offset_right = (
736	                    causal_row_offset + 1 + self.window_size_right
737	                    if const_expr(self.window_size_right is not None)
738	                    else None
739	                )
740	                local_row_offset_left = (
741	                    causal_row_offset - self.window_size_left
742	                    if const_expr(self.window_size_left is not None)
743	                    else None
744	                )
745	                if const_expr(self.window_size_right is not None):
746	                    col_limit_right = row_idx + local_row_offset_right
747	                else:
748	                    col_limit_right = self.tile_n
749	                if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
750	                    col_limit_right = cutlass.min(col_limit_right, seqlenk_col_limit)
751	                col_limit_left = (
752	                    row_idx + local_row_offset_left
753	                    if const_expr(self.window_size_left is not None)
754	                    else 0
755	                )
756	                if const_expr(not r2p):
757	                    # if cute.arch.thread_idx()[0] == 0 or cute.arch.thread_idx()[0] == 128: cute.printf("m_block = {}, n_block = {}, row_idx = {}, causal_row_offset = {}, col_limit_right = {}, col_limit_left = {}", m_block, n_block, row_idx, causal_row_offset, col_limit_right, col_limit_left)
758	                    for i in cutlass.range(cute.size(tScS_t2r.shape), unroll_full=True):
759	                        col_idx = tScS_t2r[i][1]
760	                        acc_S[i] = (
761	                            -Float32.inf
762	                            if col_idx >= col_limit_right or col_idx < col_limit_left
763	                            else acc_S[i]
764	                        )
765	                else:
766	                    # Dual-bound R2P masking for SM100.
767	                    # Masks elements where: NOT (col_limit_left <= col < col_limit_right)
768	
769	                    def mask_gen_fn(s: int) -> Uint32:
770	                        return r2p_bitmask_below(col_limit_right, s) & r2p_bitmask_above(
771	                            col_limit_left, s
772	                        )
773	
774	                    mask_r2p_lambda(acc_S, mask_gen_fn, rank1=True)
775	
776	    @cute.jit
777	    def apply_mask_sm100_transposed(
778	        self,
779	        acc_S: cute.Tensor,
780	        tScS_t2r: cute.Tensor,
781	        t0ScS_t2r: cute.Tensor,
782	        m_block: cutlass.Int32,
783	        n_block: cutlass.Int32,
784	        mask_seqlen: cutlass.Constexpr,
785	        mask_causal: cutlass.Constexpr,
786	        mask_local: cutlass.Constexpr,
787	        mask_mod: cutlass.Constexpr[Optional[Callable]] = None,
788	        batch_idx: Int32 = None,
789	        head_idx: Int32 = None,
790	        aux_data: AuxData = AuxData(),
791	        fastdiv_mods=(None, None),
792	        is_full_block: bool = False,
793	        check_m_boundary: bool = True,
794	    ) -> None:
795	        """
796	        Backward pass: mask S = K @ Q.T where n_block tiles seqlen_k and m_block tiles seqlen_q.
797	
798	        Coordinate convention:
799	        - ROW corresponds to Q (m_block)
800	        - COL corresponds to KV (n_block)
801	
802	        is_full_block: If True, skip mask_mod (all elements valid). Only apply seqlen masking.
803	        check_m_boundary: If False, skip seqlen_q boundary check (optimization for non-boundary m_blocks).
804	                          When iterating m_blocks in forward order, only the last m_block may be partial.
805	        """
806	        assert not (mask_causal and mask_local), "mask_causal and mask_local cannot be both True"
807	        ROW = 0 if const_expr(not self.swap_AB) else 1
808	        COL = 1 if const_expr(not self.swap_AB) else 0
809	        # assert t0ScS_t2r[0][COL] == 0, "col0 == 0" # tmp comment for 2-cta bwd
810	        thr_col_offset = tScS_t2r[0][COL]
811	        seqlenk_col_limit = self.seqlen_k - n_block * self.tile_n - thr_col_offset
812	
813	        if const_expr(not mask_causal and not mask_local and mask_mod is not None):
814	            # Block sparse case with mask_mod (backward)
815	            #
816	            # Coordinate convention: ROW → Q (m_block), COL → KV (n_block).
817	            # These already account for swap_AB.
818	            #
819	            # FULL blocks: mask_mod returns True for all elements, so skip it.
820	            #   Still need seqlen bounds check (elements may be OOB on last m_block).
821	            # PARTIAL blocks: apply mask_mod element-wise, then seqlen bounds.
822	            if is_full_block:
823	                if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
824	                    if seqlenk_col_limit <= 0:
825	                        # Entire tile is OOB for K
826	                        for i in cutlass.range(cute.size(acc_S.shape), unroll_full=True):
827	                            acc_S[i] = -cutlass.Float32.inf
828	                    elif check_m_boundary:
829	                        # Last m_block: check Q and K boundaries
830	                        ncol = const_expr(cute.size(tScS_t2r.shape))
831	                        for i in cutlass.range_constexpr(ncol):
832	                            row_coord = tScS_t2r[i][ROW]
833	                            col_coord = tScS_t2r[i][COL]
834	                            global_q = row_coord + m_block * self.tile_m
835	                            global_kv = col_coord + n_block * self.tile_n
836	                            q_out_of_bounds = global_q >= self.seqlen_q
837	                            kv_out_of_bounds = global_kv >= self.seqlen_k
838	                            out_of_bounds = q_out_of_bounds or kv_out_of_bounds
839	                            acc_S[i] = -cutlass.Float32.inf if out_of_bounds else acc_S[i]
840	            else:
841	                # Partial block
842	                has_fastdiv = const_expr(
843	                    fastdiv_mods is not None
844	                    and fastdiv_mods[0] is not None
845	                    and fastdiv_mods[1] is not None
846	                )
847	                wrap_aux_indices = const_expr(
848	                    has_fastdiv and mask_seqlen and const_expr(aux_data.tensors is not None)
849	                )
850	                batch_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(batch_idx, cutlass.Int32)
851	                head_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(head_idx, cutlass.Int32)
852	
853	                ncol = const_expr(cute.size(tScS_t2r.shape))
854	                for i in cutlass.range_constexpr(ncol):
855	                    row_coord = tScS_t2r[i][ROW]
856	                    col_coord = tScS_t2r[i][COL]
857	                    global_q = row_coord + m_block * self.tile_m
858	                    global_kv = col_coord + n_block * self.tile_n
859	
860	                    q_idx_for_mod = global_q
861	                    kv_idx_for_mod = global_kv
862	                    if const_expr(wrap_aux_indices):
863	                        _, q_idx_for_mod = divmod(global_q, fastdiv_mods[0])
864	                        _, kv_idx_for_mod = divmod(global_kv, fastdiv_mods[1])
865	
866	                    q_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(q_idx_for_mod, cutlass.Int32)
867	                    kv_idx_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(kv_idx_for_mod, cutlass.Int32)
868	
869	                    mask_value = call_mask_mod(
870	                        mask_mod,
871	                        batch_idx_ssa,
872	                        head_idx_ssa,
873	                        q_idx_ssa,
874	                        kv_idx_ssa,
875	                        self.seqlen_info,
876	                        aux_data,
877	                    )
878	                    cond = cutlass.Boolean(utils.ssa_to_scalar(mask_value))
879	                    acc_S[i] = acc_S[i] if cond else -cutlass.Float32.inf
880	
881	                    if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
882	                        # check_m_boundary=False skips q check for non-boundary m_blocks
883	                        q_out_of_bounds = check_m_boundary and (global_q >= self.seqlen_q)
884	                        kv_out_of_bounds = global_kv >= self.seqlen_k
885	                        out_of_bounds = q_out_of_bounds or kv_out_of_bounds
886	                        acc_S[i] = -cutlass.Float32.inf if out_of_bounds else acc_S[i]
887	
888	        elif const_expr(not mask_causal and not mask_local):
889	            if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
890	                if seqlenk_col_limit <= 0:
891	                    for i in cutlass.range(cute.size(acc_S.shape), unroll_full=True):
892	                        acc_S[i] = -cutlass.Float32.inf
893	        else:  # Causal or local
894	            thr_row_offset = tScS_t2r[0][ROW]
895	            seqlenq_row_limit = self.seqlen_q - m_block * self.tile_m - thr_row_offset
896	            causal_offset = seqlenq_row_limit - seqlenk_col_limit
897	            if const_expr(mask_causal):
898	                # tidx = cute.arch.thread_idx()[0] % 256
899	                # if tidx < 32:
900	                #     cute.printf("tidx = {}, {} {}, {} {}", tidx, tScS_t2r[0][0], tScS_t2r[0][1], tScS_t2r[1][0], tScS_t2r[1][1])
901	                row_limit_top = causal_offset
902	                if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
903	                    # If col is beyond the column limit, we want to mask out the entire
904	                    # column, by setting row limit to be self.tile_m.
905	                    if seqlenk_col_limit <= 0:
906	                        row_limit_top = self.tile_m
907	                r2p = True
908	                if const_expr(not r2p):
909	                    for i in cutlass.range(cute.size(acc_S.shape), unroll_full=True):
910	                        acc_S[i] = (
911	                            -cutlass.Float32.inf if t0ScS_t2r[i][ROW] < row_limit_top else acc_S[i]
912	                        )
913	                else:
914	                    num_rep = cute.size(tScS_t2r, mode=[0])  # 16 or 32
915	                    num_wg = 2
916	                    row_limit = row_to_r2p_idx(row_limit_top, num_rep, num_wg)
917	                    mask_r2p_lambda(
918	                        acc_S,
919	                        lambda s: r2p_bitmask_above(row_limit, s),
920	                        rank1=True,
921	                    )
922	            else:
923	                if const_expr(self.window_size_right is not None):
924	                    row_limit_top = causal_offset - self.window_size_right
925	                else:
926	                    row_limit_top = 0
927	                if const_expr(self.window_size_left is not None):
928	                    row_limit_bot = causal_offset + self.window_size_left
929	                if const_expr(mask_seqlen):
930	                    if seqlenk_col_limit <= 0:
931	                        row_limit_top = self.tile_m
932	                r2p = True
933	                if const_expr(not r2p):
934	                    for i in cutlass.range(cute.size(acc_S.shape), unroll_full=True):
935	                        row_idx = t0ScS_t2r[i][ROW]
936	                        local_mask = row_idx < row_limit_top
937	                        if const_expr(self.window_size_left is not None):
938	                            local_mask |= row_idx > row_limit_bot
939	                        acc_S[i] = -cutlass.Float32.inf if local_mask else acc_S[i]
940	                else:
941	
942	                    def mask_gen_fn(s: int) -> Uint32:
943	                        num_rep = cute.size(tScS_t2r, mode=[0])
944	                        num_wg = 2
945	
946	                        row_limit = row_to_r2p_idx(row_limit_top, num_rep, num_wg)
947	                        mask = r2p_bitmask_above(row_limit, s)
948	
949	                        if const_expr(self.window_size_left is not None):
950	                            row_limit_bottom = row_to_r2p_idx(row_limit_bot + 1, num_rep, num_wg)
951	                            mask = mask & r2p_bitmask_below(row_limit_bottom, s)
952	
953	                        return mask
954	
955	                    mask_r2p_lambda(
956	                        acc_S,
957	                        mask_gen_fn,
958	                        rank1=True,
959	                    )
960	
961	
962	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
963	# SM100 FMHA fused-mask policy layer (separate from generic mask primitives).
964	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
965	
966	
967	class Sm100MaskEnum(enum.Enum):
968	    """Enumeration of mask types for FMHA operations.
969	
970	    - RESIDUAL_MASK: Residual mask for handling variable sequence lengths
971	    - WINDOW_MASK: Window mask for attention which also includes causal and no mask
972	    - WINDOW_MASK_INFERENCE: Same as the window mask, but has the limitation that the end of q is aligned with the end of k
973	    - WINDOW_MASK_BWD: Window mask for backward pass
974	    - WINDOW_MASK_BWD_INFERENCE: Same as the window mask for backward pass, but has the limitation that the end of q is aligned with the end of k
975	    """
976	
977	    NO_MASK = enum.auto()
978	    RESIDUAL_MASK = enum.auto()
979	    CAUSAL_MASK = enum.auto()
980	    WINDOW_MASK = enum.auto()
981	    WINDOW_MASK_INFERENCE = enum.auto()
982	    # Deprecated the following types
983	    WINDOW_MASK_BWD = enum.auto()
984	    WINDOW_MASK_BWD_INFERENCE = enum.auto()
985	    RESIDUAL_MASK_BWD = enum.auto()
986	
987	
988	class Sm100FusedMask:
989	    """A fused mask implementation for FMHA operations.
990	
991	    This class handles different types of attention masks including no mask,
992	    residual mask for variable sequence lengths, and causal mask for
993	    autoregressive attention patterns.
994	
995	    The class provides methods to:
996	    - Calculate trip counts for different mask types
997	    - Apply masks to attention scores
998	    - Handle masked and unmasked trip calculations
999	    """
1000	
1001	    def get_trip_count(
1002	        mask_type: Sm100MaskEnum,
1003	        blk_coord: cute.Coord,
1004	        tile_shape: cute.Shape,
1005	        seqlen_q: Int32,
1006	        seqlen_k: Int32,
1007	        window_size_left: Optional[Int32] = None,
1008	        window_size_right: Optional[Int32] = None,
1009	    ) -> Int32:
1010	        """
1011	        Calculate the number of trips needed for the current block.
1012	
1013	        The trip count depends on the mask type and the block coordinates.
1014	        For causal masks, it considers the autoregressive constraint.
1015	
1016	        :param mask_type: Type of mask to use
1017	        :type mask_type: utils.Sm100MaskEnum
1018	        :param blk_coord: Block coordinates.
1019	        :type blk_coord: cute.Coord
1020	        :param tile_shape: Shape of the tile.
1021	        :type tile_shape: cute.Shape
1022	        :param seqlen_q: Query sequence length for attention computation.
1023	        :type seqlen_q: Int32
1024	        :param seqlen_k: Key sequence length for attention computation.
1025	        :type seqlen_k: Int32
1026	        :param window_size_left: Left-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1027	        :type window_size_left: Optional[Int32]
1028	        :param window_size_right: Right-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1029	        :type window_size_right: Optional[Int32]
1030	
1031	        :return: Number of trips needed.
1032	        :rtype: Int32
1033	        """
1034	        result = 0
1035	        offset = 0
1036	        if cutlass.const_expr(mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_INFERENCE):
1037	            offset = seqlen_k - seqlen_q
1038	        if cutlass.const_expr(mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_BWD_INFERENCE):
1039	            offset = seqlen_q - seqlen_k
1040	        if cutlass.const_expr(mask_type == Sm100MaskEnum.RESIDUAL_MASK):
1041	            result = cute.ceil_div(seqlen_k, tile_shape[1])
1042	        if cutlass.const_expr(mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.RESIDUAL_MASK_BWD):
1043	            result = cute.ceil_div(seqlen_q, tile_shape[0])
1044	        if cutlass.const_expr(
1045	            mask_type == Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK
1046	            or mask_type == Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_INFERENCE
1047	        ):
1048	            if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_right is None):
1049	                result = cute.ceil_div(seqlen_k, tile_shape[1])
1050	            else:
1051	                max_idx_q = (blk_coord[0] + 1) * tile_shape[0]
1052	                idx_k = max_idx_q + offset + window_size_right
1053	                tmp_blocks_k = cute.ceil_div(idx_k, tile_shape[1])
1054	                max_blocks_k = cute.ceil_div(seqlen_k, tile_shape[1])
1055	                result = dsl_min(max_blocks_k, tmp_blocks_k)
1056	        if cutlass.const_expr(
1057	            mask_type == Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_BWD
1058	            or mask_type == Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_BWD_INFERENCE
1059	        ):
1060	            if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_left is None):
1061	                result = cute.ceil_div(seqlen_q, tile_shape[0])
1062	            else:
1063	                max_idx_k = (blk_coord[1] + 1) * tile_shape[1]
1064	                idx_k = max_idx_k + offset + window_size_left
1065	                tmp_blocks_q = cute.ceil_div(idx_k, tile_shape[0])
1066	                max_blocks_q = cute.ceil_div(seqlen_q, tile_shape[0])
1067	                result = dsl_min(max_blocks_q, tmp_blocks_q)
1068	        start_block = Sm100FusedMask.get_trip_start(
1069	            mask_type,
1070	            blk_coord,
1071	            tile_shape,
1072	            seqlen_q,
1073	            seqlen_k,
1074	            window_size_left,
1075	            window_size_right,
1076	        )
1077	        result = result - start_block
1078	        return result
1079	
1080	    @cute.jit
1081	    def get_trip_start_count_via_block_info(
1082	        blk_coord: cute.Coord,
1083	        tile_shape: cute.Shape,
1084	        seqlen_q: Int32,
1085	        seqlen_k: Int32,
1086	        is_causal: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
1087	        is_local: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
1088	        window_size_left: Optional[Int32] = None,
1089	        window_size_right: Optional[Int32] = None,
1090	    ) -> Tuple[Int32, Int32]:
1091	        block_info = BlockInfo(
1092	            tile_m=tile_shape[0],
1093	            tile_n=tile_shape[1],
1094	            is_causal=is_causal,
1095	            is_local=is_local and not is_causal,
1096	            window_size_left=window_size_left,
1097	            window_size_right=window_size_right,
1098	        )
1099	
1100	        seqlen_info = SeqlenInfoQK(
1101	            offset_q=Int32(0),
1102	            offset_k=Int32(0),
1103	            padded_offset_q=Int32(0),
1104	            padded_offset_k=Int32(0),
1105	            seqlen_q=seqlen_q,
1106	            seqlen_k=seqlen_k,
1107	            m_block_offset=Int32(0),
1108	            block_idx_offset=Int32(0),
1109	            num_n_blocks=cute.ceil_div(seqlen_k, tile_shape[1]),
1110	            has_cu_seqlens_q=False,
1111	            has_cu_seqlens_k=False,
1112	            has_seqused_q=False,
1113	            has_seqused_k=False,
1114	        )
1115	        n_block_min, n_block_max = block_info.get_n_block_min_max(seqlen_info, blk_coord[0])
1116	        return n_block_min, n_block_max - n_block_min
1117	
1118	    @cute.jit
1119	    def get_trip_mask_bounds_via_block_info(
1120	        blk_coord: cute.Coord,
1121	        tile_shape: cute.Shape,
1122	        seqlen_q: Int32,
1123	        seqlen_k: Int32,
1124	        is_causal: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
1125	        is_local: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
1126	        window_size_left: Optional[Int32] = None,
1127	        window_size_right: Optional[Int32] = None,
1128	    ) -> Tuple[Int32, Int32]:
1129	        """Return SM100-style mask boundaries for dense iteration.
1130	
1131	        Returns:
1132	          - n_block_min_causal_local_mask: right-side masked region start
1133	          - n_block_min_before_local_mask: start of fully unmasked middle region
1134	        """
1135	        block_info = BlockInfo(
1136	            tile_m=tile_shape[0],
1137	            tile_n=tile_shape[1],
1138	            is_causal=is_causal,
1139	            is_local=is_local and not is_causal,
1140	            window_size_left=window_size_left,
1141	            window_size_right=window_size_right,
1142	        )
1143	        seqlen_info = SeqlenInfoQK(
1144	            offset_q=Int32(0),
1145	            offset_k=Int32(0),
1146	            padded_offset_q=Int32(0),
1147	            padded_offset_k=Int32(0),
1148	            seqlen_q=seqlen_q,
1149	            seqlen_k=seqlen_k,
1150	            m_block_offset=Int32(0),
1151	            block_idx_offset=Int32(0),
1152	            num_n_blocks=cute.ceil_div(seqlen_k, tile_shape[1]),
1153	            has_cu_seqlens_q=False,
1154	            has_cu_seqlens_k=False,
1155	            has_seqused_q=False,
1156	            has_seqused_k=False,
1157	        )
1158	        n_block_min, _ = block_info.get_n_block_min_max(seqlen_info, blk_coord[0])
1159	        n_block_min_causal_local_mask = block_info.get_n_block_min_causal_local_mask(
1160	            seqlen_info, blk_coord[0], n_block_min
1161	        )
1162	        n_block_min_before_local_mask = block_info.get_n_block_min_before_local_mask(
1163	            seqlen_info, blk_coord[0], n_block_min
1164	        )
1165	        return n_block_min_causal_local_mask, n_block_min_before_local_mask
1166	
1167	    @cute.jit
1168	    def get_trip_start(
1169	        mask_type: Sm100MaskEnum,
1170	        blk_coord: cute.Coord,
1171	        tile_shape: cute.Shape,
1172	        seqlen_q: Int32,
1173	        seqlen_k: Int32,
1174	        window_size_left: Optional[Int32] = None,
1175	        window_size_right: Optional[Int32] = None,
1176	    ) -> Int32:
1177	        """
1178	        Get the start of the trip for the current block.
1179	
1180	        :param mask_type: Type of mask to use
1181	        :type mask_type: utils.Sm100MaskEnum
1182	        :param blk_coord: Block coordinates.
1183	        :type blk_coord: cute.Coord
1184	        :param tile_shape: Shape of the tile.
1185	        :type tile_shape: cute.Shape
1186	        :param seqlen_q: Query sequence length for attention computation.
1187	        :type seqlen_q: Int32
1188	        :param seqlen_k: Key sequence length for attention computation.
1189	        :type seqlen_k: Int32
1190	        :param window_size_left: Left-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1191	        :type window_size_left: Optional[Int32]
1192	        :param window_size_right: Right-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1193	        :type window_size_right: Optional[Int32]
1194	        """
1195	        result = 0
1196	        offset = 0
1197	        if cutlass.const_expr(mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_INFERENCE):
1198	            offset = seqlen_k - seqlen_q
1199	        if cutlass.const_expr(mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_BWD_INFERENCE):
1200	            offset = seqlen_q - seqlen_k
1201	        if cutlass.const_expr(
1202	            mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK
1203	            or mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_INFERENCE
1204	        ):
1205	            if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_left is not None):
1206	                min_idx_q = blk_coord[0] * tile_shape[0]
1207	                idx_k = min_idx_q + offset - window_size_left
1208	                tmp_blocks_k = idx_k // tile_shape[1]
1209	                result = max(tmp_blocks_k, result)
1210	        if cutlass.const_expr(
1211	            mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_BWD
1212	            or mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_BWD_INFERENCE
1213	        ):
1214	            if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_right is not None):
1215	                min_idx_k = blk_coord[1] * tile_shape[1]
1216	                idx_q = min_idx_k + offset - window_size_right
1217	                tmp_blocks_q = idx_q // tile_shape[0]
1218	                result = max(tmp_blocks_q, result)
1219	        return result

1221	    @cute.jit
1222	    def get_leading_mask_id(
1223	        mask_type: Sm100MaskEnum,
1224	        blk_coord: cute.Coord,
1225	        tile_shape: cute.Shape,
1226	        seqlen_q: Int32,
1227	        seqlen_k: Int32,
1228	        window_size_left: Optional[Int32] = None,
1229	        window_size_right: Optional[Int32] = None,
1230	    ) -> Tuple[Int32, Int32]:
1231	        """
1232	        Get the begin and end tile idx for the leading mask.
1233	
1234	        :param mask_type: Type of mask to use
1235	        :type mask_type: utils.Sm100MaskEnum
1236	        :param blk_coord: Block coordinates.
1237	        :type blk_coord: cute.Coord
1238	        :param tile_shape: Shape of the tile.
1239	        :type tile_shape: cute.Shape
1240	        :param seqlen_q: Query sequence length for attention computation.
1241	        :type seqlen_q: Int32
1242	        :param seqlen_k: Key sequence length for attention computation.
1243	        :type seqlen_k: Int32
1244	        :param window_size_left: Left-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1245	        :type window_size_left: Optional[Int32]
1246	        :param window_size_right: Right-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1247	        :type window_size_right: Optional[Int32]
1248	
1249	        :return: Tuple of (begin, end) tile idx for the leading mask.
1250	        :rtype: Tuple[Int32, Int32]
1251	        """
1252	        offset = 0
1253	        if cutlass.const_expr(mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_INFERENCE):
1254	            offset = seqlen_k - seqlen_q
1255	        if cutlass.const_expr(mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_BWD_INFERENCE):
1256	            offset = seqlen_q - seqlen_k
1257	        leading_mask_begin = Sm100FusedMask.get_trip_start(
1258	            mask_type,
1259	            blk_coord,
1260	            tile_shape,
1261	            seqlen_q,
1262	            seqlen_k,
1263	            window_size_left,
1264	            window_size_right,
1265	        )
1266	        trip_count = Sm100FusedMask.get_trip_count(
1267	            mask_type,
1268	            blk_coord,
1269	            tile_shape,
1270	            seqlen_q,
1271	            seqlen_k,
1272	            window_size_left,
1273	            window_size_right,
1274	        )
1275	
1276	        leading_mask_end = leading_mask_begin
1277	        if cutlass.const_expr(
1278	            mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK
1279	            or mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_INFERENCE
1280	        ):
1281	            if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_left is not None):
1282	                min_idx_q = (blk_coord[0] + 1) * tile_shape[0] + offset - window_size_left
1283	                leading_mask_end = dsl_min(
1284	                    cute.ceil_div(min_idx_q, tile_shape[1]) - 1,
1285	                    trip_count + leading_mask_begin - 1,
1286	                )
1287	            else:
1288	                leading_mask_end = leading_mask_begin - 1
1289	        elif cutlass.const_expr(
1290	            mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_BWD
1291	            or mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_BWD_INFERENCE
1292	        ):
1293	            if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_right is not None):
1294	                min_idx_k = (blk_coord[1] + 1) * tile_shape[1] + offset - window_size_right
1295	                leading_mask_end = cute.ceil_div(min_idx_k, tile_shape[0]) - 1
1296	            else:
1297	                leading_mask_end = leading_mask_begin - 1
1298	        return leading_mask_begin, leading_mask_end

1300	    @cute.jit
1301	    def get_trailing_mask_id(
1302	        mask_type: Sm100MaskEnum,
1303	        blk_coord: cute.Coord,
1304	        tile_shape: cute.Shape,
1305	        seqlen_q: Int32,
1306	        seqlen_k: Int32,
1307	        window_size_left: Optional[Int32] = None,
1308	        window_size_right: Optional[Int32] = None,
1309	    ) -> Tuple[Optional[Int32], Optional[Int32]]:
1310	        """
1311	        Get the begin and end tile idx for the trailing mask.
1312	
1313	        :param mask_type: Type of mask to use
1314	        :type mask_type: utils.Sm100MaskEnum
1315	        :param blk_coord: Block coordinates.
1316	        :type blk_coord: cute.Coord
1317	        :param tile_shape: Shape of the tile.
1318	        :type tile_shape: cute.Shape
1319	        :param seqlen_q: Query sequence length for attention computation.
1320	        :type seqlen_q: Int32
1321	        :param seqlen_k: Key sequence length for attention computation.
1322	        :type seqlen_k: Int32
1323	        :param window_size_left: Left-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1324	        :type window_size_left: Optional[Int32]
1325	        :param window_size_right: Right-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1326	        :type window_size_right: Optional[Int32]
1327	
1328	        :return: Tuple of (begin, end) tile idx for the trailing mask.
1329	        :rtype: Tuple[Int32, Int32]
1330	        """
1331	        offset = 0
1332	        if cutlass.const_expr(mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_INFERENCE):
1333	            offset = seqlen_k - seqlen_q
1334	        if cutlass.const_expr(mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_BWD_INFERENCE):
1335	            offset = seqlen_q - seqlen_k
1336	        trip_start = Sm100FusedMask.get_trip_start(
1337	            mask_type,
1338	            blk_coord,
1339	            tile_shape,
1340	            seqlen_q,
1341	            seqlen_k,
1342	            window_size_left,
1343	            window_size_right,
1344	        )
1345	        trip_count = Sm100FusedMask.get_trip_count(
1346	            mask_type,
1347	            blk_coord,
1348	            tile_shape,
1349	            seqlen_q,
1350	            seqlen_k,
1351	            window_size_left,
1352	            window_size_right,
1353	        )
1354	
1355	        trailing_mask_begin, trailing_mask_end = None, None
1356	        if cutlass.const_expr(
1357	            mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK
1358	            or mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_INFERENCE
1359	        ):
1360	            if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_right is not None):
1361	                min_idx_q = blk_coord[0] * tile_shape[0] + offset + window_size_right
1362	                trailing_mask_begin = dsl_min(
1363	                    min_idx_q // tile_shape[1], trip_count + trip_start - 1
1364	                )
1365	                trailing_mask_end = trip_count + trip_start - 1
1366	            else:
1367	                # last tile, we always apply mask on it regardless whether it's a residual tile
1368	                trailing_mask_begin = trip_count + trip_start - 1
1369	                trailing_mask_end = trip_count + trip_start - 1
1370	        else:
1371	            if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_left is not None):
1372	                min_idx_k = blk_coord[1] * tile_shape[1] + offset + window_size_left + 1
1373	                max_idx_k = (blk_coord[1] + 1) * tile_shape[1] + offset + window_size_left
1374	                trailing_mask_begin = dsl_min(
1375	                    cute.ceil_div(min_idx_k, tile_shape[0]) - 1,
1376	                    trip_count + trip_start - 1,
1377	                )
1378	                trailing_mask_end = dsl_min(
1379	                    cute.ceil_div(max_idx_k, tile_shape[0]) - 1,
1380	                    trip_count + trip_start - 1,
1381	                )
1382	            else:
1383	                # last tile, we always apply mask on it regardless whether it's a residual tile
1384	                trailing_mask_begin = trip_count + trip_start - 1
1385	                trailing_mask_end = trip_count + trip_start - 1
1386	
1387	        return trailing_mask_begin, trailing_mask_end

1389	    @cute.jit
1390	    def get_masked_leading_count(
1391	        mask_type: Sm100MaskEnum,
1392	        blk_coord: cute.Coord,
1393	        tile_shape: cute.Shape,
1394	        seqlen_q: Int32,
1395	        seqlen_k: Int32,
1396	        window_size_left: Optional[Int32] = None,
1397	        window_size_right: Optional[Int32] = None,
1398	    ) -> Int32:
1399	        """
1400	        Calculate the number of masked trips for the leading mask.
1401	
1402	        This is used for blocks that need special handling due to masking.
1403	
1404	        :param mask_type: Type of mask to use
1405	        :type mask_type: utils.Sm100MaskEnum
1406	        :param blk_coord: Block coordinates.
1407	        :type blk_coord: cute.Coord
1408	        :param tile_shape: Shape of the tile.
1409	        :type tile_shape: cute.Shape
1410	        :param seqlen_q: Query sequence length for attention computation.
1411	        :type seqlen_q: Int32
1412	        :param seqlen_k: Key sequence length for attention computation.
1413	        :type seqlen_k: Int32
1414	        :param window_size_left: Left-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1415	        :type window_size_left: Optional[Int32]
1416	        :param window_size_right: Right-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1417	        :type window_size_right: Optional[Int32]
1418	
1419	        :return: Number of masked trips.
1420	        :rtype: Int32
1421	        """
1422	        result = 0
1423	        if cutlass.const_expr(
1424	            mask_type is not Sm100MaskEnum.RESIDUAL_MASK
1425	            and mask_type is not Sm100MaskEnum.RESIDUAL_MASK_BWD
1426	        ):
1427	            if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_left is not None or window_size_right is not None):
1428	                leading_mask_begin, leading_mask_end = Sm100FusedMask.get_leading_mask_id(
1429	                    mask_type,
1430	                    blk_coord,
1431	                    tile_shape,
1432	                    seqlen_q,
1433	                    seqlen_k,
1434	                    window_size_left,
1435	                    window_size_right,
1436	                )
1437	                result = max(leading_mask_end - leading_mask_begin + 1, 0)
1438	
1439	        return result

1441	    @cute.jit
1442	    def get_masked_trailing_count(
1443	        mask_type: Sm100MaskEnum,
1444	        blk_coord: cute.Coord,
1445	        tile_shape: cute.Shape,
1446	        seqlen_q: Int32,
1447	        seqlen_k: Int32,
1448	        window_size_left: Optional[Int32] = None,
1449	        window_size_right: Optional[Int32] = None,
1450	        rem_count: Optional[Int32] = 0,
1451	    ) -> Int32:
1452	        """
1453	        Calculate the number of masked trips for the trailing mask.
1454	
1455	        This is used for blocks that need special handling due to masking.
1456	
1457	        :param mask_type: Type of mask to use
1458	        :type mask_type: utils.Sm100MaskEnum
1459	        :param blk_coord: Block coordinates.
1460	        :type blk_coord: cute.Coord
1461	        :param tile_shape: Shape of the tile.
1462	        :type tile_shape: cute.Shape
1463	        :param seqlen_q: Query sequence length for attention computation.
1464	        :type seqlen_q: Int32
1465	        :param seqlen_k: Key sequence length for attention computation.
1466	        :type seqlen_k: Int32
1467	        :param window_size_left: Left-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1468	        :type window_size_left: Optional[Int32]
1469	        :param window_size_right: Right-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1470	        :type window_size_right: Optional[Int32]
1471	        :param rem_count: Remaining count from previous calculations.
1472	        :type rem_count: Int32
1473	
1474	        :return: Number of masked trips.
1475	        :rtype: Int32
1476	        """
1477	        result = 0
1478	
1479	        if cutlass.const_expr(
1480	            mask_type is not Sm100MaskEnum.RESIDUAL_MASK
1481	            and mask_type is not Sm100MaskEnum.RESIDUAL_MASK_BWD
1482	        ):
1483	            if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_left is not None or window_size_right is not None):
1484	                trailing_mask_begin, trailing_mask_end = Sm100FusedMask.get_trailing_mask_id(
1485	                    mask_type,
1486	                    blk_coord,
1487	                    tile_shape,
1488	                    seqlen_q,
1489	                    seqlen_k,
1490	                    window_size_left,
1491	                    window_size_right,
1492	                )
1493	                leading_mask_begin, leading_mask_end = Sm100FusedMask.get_leading_mask_id(
1494	                    mask_type,
1495	                    blk_coord,
1496	                    tile_shape,
1497	                    seqlen_q,
1498	                    seqlen_k,
1499	                    window_size_left,
1500	                    window_size_right,
1501	                )
1502	                if cutlass.const_expr(
1503	                    trailing_mask_begin is not None and trailing_mask_end is not None
1504	                ):
1505	                    if trailing_mask_begin <= leading_mask_end:
1506	                        result = max(trailing_mask_end - leading_mask_end, 0)
1507	                    else:
1508	                        result = max(trailing_mask_end - trailing_mask_begin + 1, 0)
1509	        else:
1510	            if seqlen_k % tile_shape[1] != 0:
1511	                result = 1
1512	            else:
1513	                result = 0
1514	
1515	        return result + rem_count

1517	    @cute.jit
1518	    def get_unmasked_trip_count(
1519	        mask_type: Sm100MaskEnum,
1520	        blk_coord: cute.Coord,
1521	        tile_shape: cute.Shape,
1522	        seqlen_q: Int32,
1523	        seqlen_k: Int32,
1524	        window_size_left: Optional[Int32] = None,
1525	        window_size_right: Optional[Int32] = None,
1526	    ) -> Int32:
1527	        """
1528	        Calculate the number of unmasked trips for the current block.
1529	
1530	        This represents the number of trips that don't require special
1531	        masking treatment.
1532	
1533	        :param mask_type: Type of mask to use
1534	        :type mask_type: utils.Sm100MaskEnum
1535	        :param blk_coord: Block coordinates.
1536	        :type blk_coord: cute.Coord
1537	        :param tile_shape: Shape of the tile.
1538	        :type tile_shape: cute.Shape
1539	        :param seqlen_q: Query sequence length for attention computation.
1540	        :type seqlen_q: Int32
1541	        :param seqlen_k: Key sequence length for attention computation.
1542	        :type seqlen_k: Int32
1543	        :param window_size_left: Left-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1544	        :type window_size_left: Optional[Int32]
1545	        :param window_size_right: Right-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1546	        :type window_size_right: Optional[Int32]
1547	
1548	        :return: Number of unmasked trips.
1549	        :rtype: Int32
1550	        """
1551	        result = (
1552	            Sm100FusedMask.get_trip_count(
1553	                mask_type,
1554	                blk_coord,
1555	                tile_shape,
1556	                seqlen_q,
1557	                seqlen_k,
1558	                window_size_left,
1559	                window_size_right,
1560	            )
1561	            - Sm100FusedMask.get_masked_leading_count(
1562	                mask_type,
1563	                blk_coord,
1564	                tile_shape,
1565	                seqlen_q,
1566	                seqlen_k,
1567	                window_size_left,
1568	                window_size_right,
1569	            )
1570	            - Sm100FusedMask.get_masked_trailing_count(
1571	                mask_type,
1572	                blk_coord,
1573	                tile_shape,
1574	                seqlen_q,
1575	                seqlen_k,
1576	                window_size_left,
1577	                window_size_right,
1578	                0,
1579	            )
1580	        )
1581	        return result
