1583	    @cute.jit
1584	    def apply_mask(
1585	        mask_type: Sm100MaskEnum,
1586	        acc_qk: cute.Tensor,
1587	        index_qk: cute.Tensor,
1588	        seqlen_q: Int32,
1589	        seqlen_k: Int32,
1590	        window_size_left: Optional[int] = None,
1591	        window_size_right: Optional[int] = None,
1592	        index_transform: cutlass.Constexpr = lambda index_q, index_k: (
1593	            index_q,
1594	            index_k,
1595	        ),
1596	    ):
1597	        """
1598	        Apply the appropriate mask to the attention scores.
1599	
1600	        This method modifies the attention scores (acc_qk) based on the mask type
1601	        and the positions in the index tensor.
1602	
1603	        :param mask_type: Type of mask to use
1604	        :type mask_type: utils.Sm100MaskEnum
1605	        :param acc_qk: Accumulated QK attention scores tensor.
1606	        :type acc_qk: cute.Tensor
1607	        :param index_qk: Index tensor containing position information.
1608	        :type index_qk: cute.Tensor
1609	        :param seqlen_k: Key sequence length for attention computation.
1610	        :type seqlen_k: Int32
1611	        :param seqlen_q: Query sequence length for attention computation.
1612	        :type seqlen_q: Optional[int]
1613	        :param window_size_left: Left-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1614	        :type window_size_left: Optional[int]
1615	        :param window_size_right: Right-side sliding window size for attention masking.
1616	        :type window_size_right: Optional[int]
1617	        """
1618	        offset = 0
1619	        # NOTE: causal masking in this repo aligns the *end* of Q with the *end* of K
1620	        # when seqlen_k != seqlen_q (same as the test/reference implementation):
1621	        #   k_index <= q_index + (seqlen_k - seqlen_q) + window_right
1622	        # In our kernels, causal is represented by (window_left is None, window_right is not None).
1623	        if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_left is None and window_size_right is not None):
1624	            offset = seqlen_k - seqlen_q
1625	        elif cutlass.const_expr(
1626	            mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_INFERENCE
1627	            or mask_type is Sm100MaskEnum.WINDOW_MASK_BWD_INFERENCE
1628	        ):
1629	            offset = seqlen_k - seqlen_q
1630	        for i in cutlass.range_constexpr(cute.size(acc_qk), unroll_full=True):
1631	            index_q, index_k = index_transform(*index_qk[i])
1632	            if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_left is not None or window_size_right is not None):
1633	                if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_left is None):
1634	                    if index_q + offset + window_size_right < index_k:
1635	                        acc_qk[i] = -Float32.inf
1636	                    if index_k >= seqlen_k or index_q >= seqlen_q:  # residual mask
1637	                        acc_qk[i] = -Float32.inf
1638	                elif cutlass.const_expr(window_size_right is None):
1639	                    if index_q + offset - window_size_left > index_k:
1640	                        acc_qk[i] = -Float32.inf
1641	                    if index_k >= seqlen_k or index_q >= seqlen_q:  # residual mask
1642	                        acc_qk[i] = -Float32.inf
1643	                else:
1644	                    max_K_index = dsl_min(index_q + offset + window_size_right, seqlen_k)
1645	                    min_K_index = max(0, index_q + offset - window_size_left)
1646	                    if index_k > max_K_index or index_k < min_K_index:
1647	                        acc_qk[i] = -Float32.inf
1648	                    if index_k >= seqlen_k or index_q >= seqlen_q:  # residual mask
1649	                        acc_qk[i] = -Float32.inf
1650	
1651	            if cutlass.const_expr(
1652	                mask_type == Sm100MaskEnum.RESIDUAL_MASK
1653	                or mask_type == Sm100MaskEnum.RESIDUAL_MASK_BWD
1654	            ):
1655	                if index_k >= seqlen_k or index_q >= seqlen_q:
1656	                    acc_qk[i] = -Float32.inf
1657	
1658	    @cute.jit
1659	    def apply_mask_via_causal_local(
1660	        acc_qk: cute.Tensor,
1661	        index_qk: cute.Tensor,
1662	        seqlen_q: Int32,
1663	        seqlen_k: Int32,
1664	        apply_semantic_window: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = True,
1665	        is_causal: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
1666	        is_local: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
1667	        window_size_left: Optional[int] = None,
1668	        window_size_right: Optional[int] = None,
1669	        index_transform: cutlass.Constexpr = lambda index_q, index_k: (
1670	            index_q,
1671	            index_k,
1672	        ),
1673	    ):
1674	        """Apply forward mask without mask_type.
1675	
1676	        - If apply_semantic_window=True, apply causal/local window constraints.
1677	        - Always apply residual OOB masking (index_k>=seqlen_k or index_q>=seqlen_q).
1678	        """
1679	        offset = 0
1680	        if cutlass.const_expr(apply_semantic_window):
1681	            # Match WINDOW_MASK_INFERENCE semantics: end-align Q/K when lengths differ.
1682	            offset = seqlen_k - seqlen_q
1683	        for i in cutlass.range_constexpr(cute.size(acc_qk), unroll_full=True):
1684	            index_q, index_k = index_transform(*index_qk[i])
1685	            if cutlass.const_expr(apply_semantic_window):
1686	                if cutlass.const_expr(is_causal and not is_local):
1687	                    # Pure causal; tolerate both external forms:
1688	                    # - (None, None) from interface
1689	                    # - (None, 0) from fused-mask-style callers
1690	                    right = 0 if const_expr(window_size_right is None) else window_size_right
1691	                    if index_q + offset + right < index_k:
1692	                        acc_qk[i] = -Float32.inf
1693	                elif cutlass.const_expr(
1694	                    is_local or window_size_left is not None or window_size_right is not None
1695	                ):
1696	                    if cutlass.const_expr(window_size_left is None):
1697	                        if index_q + offset + window_size_right < index_k:
1698	                            acc_qk[i] = -Float32.inf
1699	                    elif cutlass.const_expr(window_size_right is None):
1700	                        if index_q + offset - window_size_left > index_k:
1701	                            acc_qk[i] = -Float32.inf
1702	                    else:
1703	                        max_K_index = dsl_min(index_q + offset + window_size_right, seqlen_k)
1704	                        min_K_index = max(0, index_q + offset - window_size_left)
1705	                        if index_k > max_K_index or index_k < min_K_index:
1706	                            acc_qk[i] = -Float32.inf
1707	            # Residual mask is always needed for boundary protection.
1708	            if index_k >= seqlen_k or index_q >= seqlen_q:
1709	                acc_qk[i] = -Float32.inf
1710

1	from typing import Optional
2	from dataclasses import dataclass
3	
4	import cutlass
5	import cutlass.cute as cute
6	from cutlass import Int32, const_expr
7	
8	from quack import copy_utils
9	
10	"""
11	This consolidates all the info related to sequence length. This is so that we can do all
12	the gmem reads once at the beginning of each tile, rather than having to repeat these reads
13	to compute various things like n_block_min, n_block_max, etc.
14	"""
15	
16	
17	@dataclass(frozen=True)
18	class SeqlenInfo:
19	    offset: Int32
20	    offset_padded: Int32
21	    seqlen: Int32
22	    has_cu_seqlens: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False
23	
24	    @staticmethod
25	    def create(
26	        batch_idx: Int32,
27	        seqlen_static: Int32,
28	        cu_seqlens: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
29	        seqused: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
30	        tile: cutlass.Constexpr[int] = 128,
31	    ):
32	        offset = 0 if const_expr(cu_seqlens is None) else cu_seqlens[batch_idx]
33	        offset_padded = (
34	            0
35	            if const_expr(cu_seqlens is None)
36	            # Add divby so that the compiler knows the alignment when moving by offset_padded
37	            else cute.assume((offset + batch_idx * tile) // tile * tile, divby=tile)
38	        )
39	        if const_expr(seqused is not None):
40	            seqlen = seqused[batch_idx]
41	        elif const_expr(cu_seqlens is not None):
42	            seqlen = cu_seqlens[batch_idx + 1] - cu_seqlens[batch_idx]
43	        else:
44	            seqlen = seqlen_static
45	        return SeqlenInfo(offset, offset_padded, seqlen, has_cu_seqlens=cu_seqlens is not None)
46	
47	    def offset_batch(
48	        self,
49	        mT: cute.Tensor,
50	        batch_idx: Int32,
51	        dim: int,
52	        padded: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
53	        multiple: int = 1,
54	    ) -> cute.Tensor:
55	        """Offset a tensor by batch index. batch dim is at position `dim`, seqlen is at dim=0."""
56	        if const_expr(not self.has_cu_seqlens):
57	            idx = (None,) * dim + (batch_idx,) + (None,) * (cute.rank(mT) - 1 - dim)
58	            return mT[idx]
59	        else:
60	            off = multiple * (self.offset if const_expr(not padded) else self.offset_padded)
61	            offset = off if const_expr(cute.rank(mT.shape[0]) == 1) else (0, off)
62	            idx = (offset,) + (None,) * (cute.rank(mT) - 1)
63	            return cute.domain_offset(idx, mT)
64	
65	
66	@dataclass(frozen=True)
67	class SeqlenInfoQK:
68	    offset_q: Int32
69	    offset_k: Int32
70	    padded_offset_q: Int32
71	    padded_offset_k: Int32
72	    seqlen_q: Int32
73	    seqlen_k: Int32
74	    m_block_offset: Int32
75	    block_idx_offset: Int32
76	    num_n_blocks: Int32
77	    has_cu_seqlens_q: cutlass.Constexpr[bool]
78	    has_cu_seqlens_k: cutlass.Constexpr[bool]
79	    has_seqused_q: cutlass.Constexpr[bool]
80	    has_seqused_k: cutlass.Constexpr[bool]
81	
82	    @staticmethod
83	    def create(
84	        batch_idx: Int32,
85	        seqlen_q_static: Int32,
86	        seqlen_k_static: Int32,
87	        mCuSeqlensQ: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
88	        mCuSeqlensK: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
89	        mSeqUsedQ: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
90	        mSeqUsedK: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
91	        mCuTotalMBlocks: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
92	        mCuBlockIdxOffsets: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
93	        tile_m: cutlass.Constexpr[Int32] = 128,
94	        tile_n: cutlass.Constexpr[Int32] = 128,
95	    ):
96	        offset_q = 0 if const_expr(mCuSeqlensQ is None) else mCuSeqlensQ[batch_idx]
97	        offset_k = 0 if const_expr(mCuSeqlensK is None) else mCuSeqlensK[batch_idx]
98	        padded_offset_q = (
99	            0
100	            if const_expr(mCuSeqlensQ is None)
101	            else cute.assume((offset_q + batch_idx * tile_m) // tile_m * tile_m, divby=tile_m)
102	        )
103	        padded_offset_k = (
104	            0
105	            if const_expr(mCuSeqlensK is None)
106	            else cute.assume((offset_k + batch_idx * tile_n) // tile_n * tile_n, divby=tile_n)
107	        )
108	        if const_expr(mSeqUsedQ is not None):
109	            seqlen_q = mSeqUsedQ[batch_idx]
110	        else:
111	            seqlen_q = (
112	                seqlen_q_static
113	                if const_expr(mCuSeqlensQ is None)
114	                else mCuSeqlensQ[batch_idx + 1] - offset_q
115	            )
116	        if const_expr(mSeqUsedK is not None):
117	            seqlen_k = mSeqUsedK[batch_idx]
118	        else:
119	            seqlen_k = (
120	                seqlen_k_static
121	                if const_expr(mCuSeqlensK is None)
122	                else mCuSeqlensK[batch_idx + 1] - offset_k
123	            )
124	        m_block_offset = 0 if const_expr(mCuTotalMBlocks is None) else mCuTotalMBlocks[batch_idx]
125	        num_n_blocks = (seqlen_k + tile_n - 1) // tile_n
126	        block_idx_offset = (
127	            mCuBlockIdxOffsets[batch_idx]
128	            if const_expr(mCuBlockIdxOffsets is not None)
129	            else m_block_offset * num_n_blocks
130	        )
131	        return SeqlenInfoQK(
132	            offset_q,
133	            offset_k,
134	            padded_offset_q,
135	            padded_offset_k,
136	            seqlen_q,
137	            seqlen_k,
138	            m_block_offset,
139	            block_idx_offset,
140	            num_n_blocks,
141	            has_cu_seqlens_q=mCuSeqlensQ is not None,
142	            has_cu_seqlens_k=mCuSeqlensK is not None,
143	            has_seqused_q=mSeqUsedQ is not None,
144	            has_seqused_k=mSeqUsedK is not None,
145	        )
146	
147	    def offset_batch_Q(
148	        self,
149	        mQ: cute.Tensor,
150	        batch_idx: Int32,
151	        dim: int,
152	        padded: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
153	        ragged: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
154	    ) -> cute.Tensor:
155	        """Seqlen must be the first dimension of mQ"""
156	        if const_expr(not ragged):
157	            if const_expr(not self.has_cu_seqlens_q):
158	                idx = (None,) * dim + (batch_idx,) + (None,) * (cute.rank(mQ) - 1 - dim)
159	                return mQ[idx]
160	            else:
161	                offset_q = self.offset_q if const_expr(not padded) else self.padded_offset_q
162	                offset_q = offset_q if const_expr(cute.rank(mQ.shape[0]) == 1) else (None, offset_q)
163	                idx = (offset_q,) + (None,) * (cute.rank(mQ) - 1)
164	                return cute.domain_offset(idx, mQ)
165	        else:
166	            if const_expr(not self.has_cu_seqlens_q):
167	                offset_q = 0
168	                idx = (None,) * dim + (batch_idx,) + (None,) * (cute.rank(mQ) - 1 - dim)
169	                mQ = mQ[idx]
170	            else:
171	                offset_q = self.offset_q if const_expr(not padded) else self.padded_offset_q
172	            if const_expr(cute.rank(mQ.shape[0]) == 1):
173	                return copy_utils.offset_ragged_tensor(
174	                    mQ, offset_q, self.seqlen_q, ragged_dim=0, ptr_shift=True
175	                )
176	            else:  # PackGQA
177	                assert cute.rank(mQ.shape[0]) == 2
178	                # Unpack before calling offset_ragged_tensor, then pack
179	                idx = ((None, None),) + (None,) * (cute.rank(mQ) - 1)
180	                mQ = mQ[idx]
181	                mQ = copy_utils.offset_ragged_tensor(
182	                    mQ, offset_q, self.seqlen_q, ragged_dim=1, ptr_shift=True
183	                )
184	                return cute.group_modes(mQ, 0, 2)
185	
186	    def offset_batch_K(
187	        self,
188	        mK: cute.Tensor,
189	        batch_idx: Int32,
190	        dim: int,
191	        padded: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
192	        ragged: cutlass.Constexpr[bool] = False,
193	        multiple: int = 1,
194	    ) -> cute.Tensor:
195	        """Seqlen must be the first dimension of mK"""
196	        if const_expr(not ragged):
197	            if const_expr(not self.has_cu_seqlens_k):
198	                idx = (None,) * dim + (batch_idx,) + (None,) * (cute.rank(mK) - 1 - dim)
199	                return mK[idx]
200	            else:
201	                offset_k = self.offset_k if const_expr(not padded) else self.padded_offset_k
202	                offset_k *= multiple
203	                idx = (offset_k,) + (None,) * (cute.rank(mK) - 1)
204	                return cute.domain_offset(idx, mK)
205	        else:
206	            if const_expr(not self.has_cu_seqlens_k):
207	                offset_k = 0
208	                idx = (None,) * dim + (batch_idx,) + (None,) * (cute.rank(mK) - 1 - dim)
209	                mK = mK[idx]
210	            else:
211	                offset_k = self.offset_k if const_expr(not padded) else self.padded_offset_k
212	                offset_k *= multiple
213	            return copy_utils.offset_ragged_tensor(
214	                mK, offset_k, self.seqlen_k, ragged_dim=0, ptr_shift=True
215	            )
216	
217	
218	@dataclass(frozen=True)
219	class SeqlenInfoQKNewK:
220	    """Sequence length info for append-KV with left-padding and new K support.
221	
222	    Extends SeqlenInfoQK with:
223	    - leftpad_k: left padding for K (tokens to skip at the start of the KV cache)
224	    - offset_k_new: offset into the new K tensor
225	    - seqlen_k_og: original K length (before appending new K), excluding leftpad
226	    - seqlen_k_new: length of new K to append
227	    - seqlen_k: total K length (seqlen_k_og + seqlen_k_new)
228	    - seqlen_rotary: position for rotary embedding computation
229	    """
230	
231	    leftpad_k: Int32
232	    offset_q: Int32
233	    offset_k: Int32
234	    offset_k_new: Int32
235	    seqlen_q: Int32
236	    seqlen_k_og: Int32
237	    seqlen_k_new: Int32
238	    seqlen_k: Int32
239	    seqlen_rotary: Int32
240	
241	    @staticmethod
242	    def create(
243	        batch_idx: Int32,
244	        seqlen_q_static: Int32,
245	        seqlen_k_static: Int32,
246	        shape_K_new_0: Int32,
247	        mCuSeqlensQ: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
248	        mCuSeqlensK: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
249	        mCuSeqlensKNew: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
250	        mSeqUsedQ: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
251	        mSeqUsedK: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
252	        mLeftpadK: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
253	        mSeqlensRotary: Optional[cute.Tensor] = None,
254	    ):
255	        leftpad_k = 0 if const_expr(mLeftpadK is None) else mLeftpadK[batch_idx]
256	        offset_q = 0 if const_expr(mCuSeqlensQ is None) else mCuSeqlensQ[batch_idx]
257	        if const_expr(mCuSeqlensK is not None):
258	            offset_k = mCuSeqlensK[batch_idx] + leftpad_k
259	        else:
260	            offset_k = leftpad_k if const_expr(mCuSeqlensQ is not None) else 0
261	        offset_k_new = 0 if const_expr(mCuSeqlensKNew is None) else mCuSeqlensKNew[batch_idx]
262	        # seqlen_q
263	        if const_expr(mSeqUsedQ is not None):
264	            seqlen_q = mSeqUsedQ[batch_idx]
265	        elif const_expr(mCuSeqlensQ is not None):
266	            seqlen_q = mCuSeqlensQ[batch_idx + 1] - mCuSeqlensQ[batch_idx]
267	        else:
268	            seqlen_q = seqlen_q_static
269	        # seqlen_k_og: original K length (excluding leftpad)
270	        if const_expr(mSeqUsedK is not None):
271	            seqlen_k_og = mSeqUsedK[batch_idx] - leftpad_k
272	        elif const_expr(mCuSeqlensK is not None):
273	            seqlen_k_og = mCuSeqlensK[batch_idx + 1] - mCuSeqlensK[batch_idx] - leftpad_k
274	        else:
275	            seqlen_k_og = (
276	                seqlen_k_static - leftpad_k
277	                if const_expr(mCuSeqlensQ is not None)
278	                else seqlen_k_static
279	            )
280	        # seqlen_k_new
281	        if const_expr(mCuSeqlensKNew is None):
282	            seqlen_k_new = 0 if const_expr(mCuSeqlensQ is None) else shape_K_new_0
283	        else:
284	            seqlen_k_new = mCuSeqlensKNew[batch_idx + 1] - mCuSeqlensKNew[batch_idx]
285	        seqlen_k = seqlen_k_og if const_expr(mCuSeqlensQ is None) else seqlen_k_og + seqlen_k_new
286	
287	        # seqlen_rotary: defaults to seqlen_k_og + leftpad_k unless explicitly provided
288	        if const_expr(mSeqlensRotary is not None):
289	            seqlen_rotary = mSeqlensRotary[batch_idx]
290	        else:
291	            seqlen_rotary = seqlen_k_og + leftpad_k
292	        return SeqlenInfoQKNewK(
293	            leftpad_k,
294	            offset_q,
295	            offset_k,
296	            offset_k_new,
297	            seqlen_q,
298	            seqlen_k_og,
299	            seqlen_k_new,
300	            seqlen_k,
301	            seqlen_rotary,
302	        )
303

1	from typing import Callable, Optional
2	
3	import random
4	import math
5	
6	import cutlass
7	import cutlass.cute as cute
8	import torch
9	
10	from flash_attn.cute import utils
11	from flash_attn.cute.block_sparsity import fast_sampling
12	
13	
14	# =============================================================================
15	# CuTe mask_mod functions (for kernel compilation)
16	# All use signature: (batch, head, m_idx, n_idx, seqlen_info, aux_tensors)
17	# =============================================================================
18	
19	# =============================================================================
20	# mask_mod functions that don't use global indices
21	# =============================================================================
22	
23	
24	@fast_sampling
25	@cute.jit
26	def cute_causal_mask(
27	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
28	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
29	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
30	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
31	    seqlen_info,
32	    aux_tensors: None,
33	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
34	    offset = seqlen_info.seqlen_k - seqlen_info.seqlen_q
35	    offset_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(offset, cutlass.Int32)
36	    return n_idx <= (m_idx + offset_ssa)
37	
38	
39	def get_cute_causal_mask(offset: int):
40	    return cute_causal_mask
41	
42	
43	def get_cute_block_causal_mask(offset: int):
44	    @fast_sampling
45	    @cute.jit
46	    def _cute_block_causal_mask(
47	        batch: cute.TensorSSA,
48	        head: cute.TensorSSA,
49	        m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
50	        n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
51	        seqlen_info,
52	        aux_tensors: None,
53	    ) -> cute.TensorSSA:
54	        offset_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(offset, cutlass.Int32)
55	        return n_idx <= (m_idx + offset_ssa)
56	
57	    return _cute_block_causal_mask
58	
59	
60	def get_cute_sliding_window_mask(window_left: int, window_right: int, offset: int):
61	    @fast_sampling
62	    @cute.jit
63	    def _cute_sliding_window_mask(
64	        batch: cute.TensorSSA,
65	        head: cute.TensorSSA,
66	        m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
67	        n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
68	        seqlen_info,
69	        aux_tensors,
70	    ) -> cute.TensorSSA:
71	        offset = seqlen_info.seqlen_k - seqlen_info.seqlen_q
72	        offset_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(offset, cutlass.Int32)
73	        window_left_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(window_left, cutlass.Int32)
74	        window_right_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(window_right, cutlass.Int32)
75	        center = m_idx + offset_ssa
76	        lower = center - window_left_ssa
77	        upper = center + window_right_ssa
78	        return (n_idx >= lower) & (n_idx <= upper)
79	
80	    return _cute_sliding_window_mask
81	
82	
83	@fast_sampling
84	@cute.jit
85	def cute_block_diagonal_mask(
86	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
87	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
88	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
89	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
90	    seqlen_info,
91	    aux_tensors,
92	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
93	    block_size_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(128, cutlass.Int32)
94	    return (m_idx // block_size_ssa) == (n_idx // block_size_ssa)
95	
96	
97	@cute.jit
98	def cute_mini_causal_mask(
99	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
100	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
101	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
102	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
103	    seqlen_info,
104	    aux_tensors,
105	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
106	    tile_size_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(128, cutlass.Int32)
107	    m_mod = m_idx % tile_size_ssa
108	    n_mod = n_idx % tile_size_ssa
109	    return m_mod >= n_mod
110	
111	
112	@fast_sampling
113	@cute.jit
114	def cute_prefix_lm_mask(
115	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
116	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
117	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
118	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
119	    seqlen_info,
120	    aux_tensors,
121	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
122	    """Prefix LM mask: first 512 tokens attend bidirectionally, rest use causal masking."""
123	    prefix_size_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(512, cutlass.Int32)
124	    both_in_prefix = (m_idx < prefix_size_ssa) & (n_idx < prefix_size_ssa)
125	    causal_part = m_idx >= n_idx
126	    return both_in_prefix | causal_part
127	
128	
129	@cute.jit
130	def cute_dilated_sliding_window_mask(
131	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
132	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
133	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
134	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
135	    seqlen_info,
136	    aux_tensors,
137	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
138	    """Dilated sliding window: every other position in a 256-position window."""
139	    window_size_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(256, cutlass.Int32)
140	    dilation_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(2, cutlass.Int32)
141	    in_window = (m_idx >= n_idx) & (m_idx - n_idx < window_size_ssa)
142	    dilated = ((m_idx - n_idx) % dilation_ssa) == utils.scalar_to_ssa(0, cutlass.Int32)
143	    return in_window & dilated
144	
145	
146	@fast_sampling
147	@cute.jit
148	def cute_document_mask(
149	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
150	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
151	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
152	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
153	    seqlen_info,
154	    aux_tensors: list,
155	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
156	    doc_id = aux_tensors[0]
157	    m_doc = utils.scalar_to_ssa(doc_id[batch[0], head[0], m_idx[0]], cutlass.Int32)
158	    n_doc = utils.scalar_to_ssa(doc_id[batch[0], head[0], n_idx[0]], cutlass.Int32)
159	    return m_doc == n_doc
160	
161	
162	@cute.jit
163	def cute_ima_mask(
164	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
165	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
166	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
167	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
168	    seqlen_info,
169	    aux_tensors,
170	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
171	    bias = aux_tensors[0]
172	    threshold = utils.scalar_to_ssa(bias[n_idx[0]], cutlass.Int32)
173	    return n_idx >= threshold
174	
175	
176	# =============================================================================
177	# mask_mod functions that use global indices (for use with variable sequence length)
178	# Global indices computed as: m_idx_global = m_idx + seqlen_info.offset_q
179	#                            n_idx_global = n_idx + seqlen_info.offset_k
180	# =============================================================================
181	
182	
183	@fast_sampling
184	@cute.jit
185	def cute_global_packed_doc_mask(
186	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
187	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
188	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
189	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
190	    seqlen_info,
191	    aux_tensors,
192	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
193	    """Document mask using globally-indexed packed 1D doc ID tensors.
194	
195	    aux_tensors[0]: doc_ids_q (total_q,) int32 — packed doc IDs for Q tokens
196	    aux_tensors[1]: doc_ids_k (total_k,) int32 — packed doc IDs for K tokens
197	    Mask: doc_ids_q[m_global] == doc_ids_k[n_global]
198	    """
199	    doc_ids_q = aux_tensors[0]
200	    doc_ids_k = aux_tensors[1]
201	
202	    offset_q = seqlen_info.offset_q
203	    m_global = m_idx + offset_q
204	    m_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
205	    m_frag.store(m_global)
206	    m_doc_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
207	    m_doc_frag[0] = doc_ids_q[m_frag[0]]
208	
209	    offset_k = seqlen_info.offset_k
210	    n_global = n_idx + offset_k
211	    n_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
212	    n_frag.store(n_global)
213	    n_doc_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
214	    n_doc_frag[0] = doc_ids_k[n_frag[0]]
215	
216	    m_doc = m_doc_frag.load()
217	    n_doc = n_doc_frag.load()
218	    return m_doc == n_doc
219	
220	
221	@fast_sampling
222	@cute.jit
223	def cute_global_ima_mask(
224	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
225	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
226	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
227	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
228	    seqlen_info,
229	    aux_tensors,
230	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
231	    """IMA-style mask using globally-indexed threshold tensor.
232	
233	    aux_tensors[0]: thresholds (total_k,) int32 — per-global-kv-position threshold
234	    Mask: n_idx >= thresholds[n_global]  (local n_idx >= globally-indexed threshold)
235	    """
236	    thresholds = aux_tensors[0]
237	
238	    offset_k = seqlen_info.offset_k
239	    n_global = n_idx + offset_k
240	    n_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
241	    n_frag.store(n_global)
242	    val_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
243	    val_frag[0] = thresholds[n_frag[0]]
244	    threshold = val_frag.load()
245	
246	    return n_idx >= threshold
247	
248	
249	@fast_sampling
250	@cute.jit
251	def cute_global_causal_window_mask(
252	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
253	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
254	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
255	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
256	    seqlen_info,
257	    aux_tensors,
258	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
259	    """Causal window mask with per-token window sizes indexed globally.
260	
261	    aux_tensors[0]: windows (total_q,) int32 — per-global-q-position window size
262	    Mask: (n_idx <= m_idx) & (m_idx - n_idx <= windows[m_global])
263	    """
264	    windows = aux_tensors[0]
265	
266	    offset_q = seqlen_info.offset_q
267	    m_global = m_idx + offset_q
268	    m_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
269	    m_frag.store(m_global)
270	    win_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
271	    win_frag[0] = windows[m_frag[0]]
272	    window = win_frag.load()
273	
274	    return (n_idx <= m_idx) & ((m_idx - n_idx) <= window)
275

278	# Eager reference functions (PyTorch/Flex Attention signatures)

281	
282	def get_flex_causal_mask(offset: int):
283	    def _flex_causal_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
284	        return kv_idx <= q_idx + offset
285	
286	    return _flex_causal_mask
287	
288	
289	def get_flex_block_causal_mask(offset: int):
290	    def _flex_block_causal_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
291	        return kv_idx <= q_idx + offset
292	
293	    return _flex_block_causal_mask
294	
295	
296	def get_flex_sliding_window_mask(window_left: int, window_right: int, offset: int):
297	    def _flex_sliding_window_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
298	        center = q_idx + offset
299	        lower = center - window_left
300	        upper = center + window_right
301	        return (kv_idx >= lower) & (kv_idx <= upper)
302	
303	    return _flex_sliding_window_mask
304	
305	
306	def flex_block_diagonal_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
307	    block_size = 128
308	    return (q_idx // block_size) == (kv_idx // block_size)
309	
310	
311	def flex_mini_causal_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
312	    return (q_idx % 128) >= (kv_idx % 128)
313	
314	
315	def flex_prefix_lm_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
316	    """Prefix LM mask: first 512 tokens attend bidirectionally, rest use causal masking."""
317	    prefix_size = 512
318	    both_in_prefix = (q_idx < prefix_size) & (kv_idx < prefix_size)
319	    causal_part = q_idx >= kv_idx
320	    return both_in_prefix | causal_part
321	
322	
323	def flex_dilated_sliding_window_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
324	    """Dilated sliding window: every other position in a 256-position window."""
325	    window_size = 256
326	    dilation = 2
327	    in_window = (q_idx >= kv_idx) & (q_idx - kv_idx < window_size)
328	    dilated = ((q_idx - kv_idx) % dilation) == 0
329	    return in_window & dilated
330	
331	
332	def flex_document_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx, doc_id):
333	    return doc_id[b, h, q_idx] == doc_id[b, h, kv_idx]
334	
335	
336	def flex_ima_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx, bias):
337	    return kv_idx >= bias[kv_idx]
338

340	# =============================================================================
341	# Flex reference factories for global-index masks (per-sequence)
342	# Each factory(seq_idx, sq, sk) -> mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx)
343	# where q_idx/kv_idx are local (0-indexed) within the sequence.
344	# =============================================================================
345	
346	
347	def global_packed_doc_flex_factory(doc_ids_q, doc_ids_k, cu_seqlens_q, cu_seqlens_k):
348	    """Factory for per-sequence flex reference of cute_global_packed_doc_mask."""
349	
350	    def factory(seq_idx, sq, sk):
351	        q_offset = cu_seqlens_q[seq_idx].item()
352	        k_offset = cu_seqlens_k[seq_idx].item()
353	
354	        def mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
355	            q_global = q_offset + q_idx
356	            k_global = k_offset + kv_idx
357	            return doc_ids_q[q_global] == doc_ids_k[k_global]
358	
359	        return mask
360	
361	    return factory
362	
363	
364	def global_ima_flex_factory(thresholds, cu_seqlens_k):
365	    """Factory for per-sequence flex reference of cute_global_ima_mask."""
366	
367	    def factory(seq_idx, sq, sk):
368	        k_offset = cu_seqlens_k[seq_idx].item()
369	
370	        def mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
371	            k_global = k_offset + kv_idx
372	            return kv_idx >= thresholds[k_global]
373	
374	        return mask
375	
376	    return factory
377	
378	
379	def global_causal_window_flex_factory(windows, cu_seqlens_q):
380	    """Factory for per-sequence flex reference of cute_global_causal_window_mask."""
381	
382	    def factory(seq_idx, sq, sk):
383	        q_offset = cu_seqlens_q[seq_idx].item()
384	
385	        def mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
386	            q_global = q_offset + q_idx
387	            window = windows[q_global]
388	            return (kv_idx <= q_idx) & ((q_idx - kv_idx) <= window)
389	
390	        return mask
391	
392	    return factory
393

395	# =============================================================================
396	# Utility functions
397	# =============================================================================
398	
399	
400	def random_doc_id_tensor(nheads, batch, seqlen_q, device="cpu"):
401	    """Generate synthetic document ids shared across heads."""
402	    doc_ids_tensor = torch.zeros(
403	        batch, nheads, seqlen_q, dtype=torch.int32, device=device
404	    )
405	    for b in range(batch):
406	        N = seqlen_q
407	        max_segments = max(1, math.ceil(math.sqrt(max(N // 4, 1))))
408	        n = random.randint(1, max_segments)
409	        n = min(n, N)
410	        cuts = sorted(random.sample(range(1, N), n - 1))
411	        lengths = [b - a for a, b in zip((0, *cuts), (*cuts, N))]
412	        base_doc_ids = torch.repeat_interleave(
413	            torch.arange(len(lengths), device=device, dtype=torch.int32),
414	            torch.tensor(lengths, device=device, dtype=torch.int32),
415	        )
416	
417	        for h in range(nheads):
418	            doc_ids_tensor[b, h, :] = base_doc_ids
419	    return doc_ids_tensor
420	
421	
422	def make_packed_doc_ids(seqlens_q, seqlens_k, device="cuda"):
423	    """Generate packed 1D doc ID tensors for Q and K for varlen global-index testing.
424	
425	    For each sequence, divides tokens into sqrt(len)-ish segments.
426	    Returns (doc_ids_q, doc_ids_k) of shape (total_q,) and (total_k,).
427	    """
428	    total_q = sum(seqlens_q)
429	    total_k = sum(seqlens_k)
430	    doc_ids_q = torch.zeros(total_q, dtype=torch.int32, device=device)
431	    doc_ids_k = torch.zeros(total_k, dtype=torch.int32, device=device)
432	
433	    q_off = 0
434	    k_off = 0
435	    for sq, sk in zip(seqlens_q, seqlens_k):
436	        # Q doc IDs
437	        n_docs = max(1, math.ceil(math.sqrt(max(sq // 4, 1))))
438	        n_docs = min(n_docs, sq)
439	        if n_docs > 1 and sq > 1:
440	            cuts = sorted(random.sample(range(1, sq), min(n_docs - 1, sq - 1)))
441	        else:
442	            cuts = []
443	        lengths = [b - a for a, b in zip((0, *cuts), (*cuts, sq))]
444	        doc_ids_q[q_off : q_off + sq] = torch.repeat_interleave(
445	            torch.arange(len(lengths), dtype=torch.int32, device=device),
446	            torch.tensor(lengths, dtype=torch.int32, device=device),
447	        )
448	
449	        # K doc IDs (same n_docs range for potential overlap)
450	        if n_docs > 1 and sk > 1:
451	            cuts_k = sorted(random.sample(range(1, sk), min(n_docs - 1, sk - 1)))
452	        else:
453	            cuts_k = []
454	        lengths_k = [b - a for a, b in zip((0, *cuts_k), (*cuts_k, sk))]
455	        doc_ids_k[k_off : k_off + sk] = torch.repeat_interleave(
456	            torch.arange(len(lengths_k), dtype=torch.int32, device=device),
457	            torch.tensor(lengths_k, dtype=torch.int32, device=device),
458	        )
459	
460	        q_off += sq
461	        k_off += sk
462	
463	    return doc_ids_q, doc_ids_k
464	
465	
466	def make_global_thresholds(seqlens_k, device="cuda"):
467	    """Generate per-global-kv-token thresholds for cute_global_ima_mask.
468	
469	    For each K token at local index i in a sequence of length sk,
470	    threshold = random value in [0, sk//2].
471	    Returns thresholds of shape (total_k,).
472	    """
473	    total_k = sum(seqlens_k)
474	    thresholds = torch.zeros(total_k, dtype=torch.int32, device=device)
475	    k_off = 0
476	    for sk in seqlens_k:
477	        for i in range(sk):
478	            thresholds[k_off + i] = random.randint(0, max(0, sk // 2))
479	        k_off += sk
480	    return thresholds
481	
482	
483	def make_global_windows(seqlens_q, device="cuda"):
484	    """Generate per-global-q-token window sizes for cute_global_causal_window_mask.
485	
486	    For Q token at local index i, window = random value in [0, i] (causal).
487	    Returns windows of shape (total_q,).
488	    """
489	    total_q = sum(seqlens_q)
490	    windows = torch.zeros(total_q, dtype=torch.int32, device=device)
491	    q_off = 0
492	    for sq in seqlens_q:
493	        for i in range(sq):
494	            windows[q_off + i] = random.randint(0, i)
495	        q_off += sq
496	    return windows
497

499	# =============================================================================
500	# Vectorized mask_mod variants (return bit-packed Uint32)
501	# =============================================================================
502	#
503	# Each variant receives shape-(vec_size,) Int32 SSAs and returns a shape-
504	# (max(1, vec_size // 32),) Uint32 TensorSSA, where bit i of element k is the
505	# mask for lane (k * 32 + i). Bodies assume lane i has idx[i] = idx[0] + i, so
506	# they compute the packed Uint32(s) in O(1) via integer/bit arithmetic on the
507	# chunk-base indices instead of evaluating per lane.
508	
509	
510	@cute.jit
511	def cute_causal_mask_vec(
512	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
513	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
514	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
515	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
516	    seqlen_info,
517	    aux_tensors: None,
518	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
519	    offset = seqlen_info.seqlen_k - seqlen_info.seqlen_q
520	    threshold = m_idx[0] + offset - n_idx[0] + cutlass.Int32(1)
521	    m = max(cutlass.Int32(32) - threshold, cutlass.Int32(0))
522	    result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
523	    result[0] = utils.shr_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(m))
524	    return result.load()
525	
526	
527	def get_cute_causal_mask_vec(offset: int):
528	    return cute_causal_mask_vec
529	
530	
531	def get_cute_sliding_window_mask_vec(window_left: int, window_right: int, offset: int):
532	    @cute.jit
533	    def _cute_sliding_window_mask_vec(
534	        batch: cute.TensorSSA,
535	        head: cute.TensorSSA,
536	        m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
537	        n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
538	        seqlen_info,
539	        aux_tensors,
540	    ) -> cute.TensorSSA:
541	        runtime_offset = seqlen_info.seqlen_k - seqlen_info.seqlen_q
542	        center = m_idx[0] + runtime_offset
543	        lo = center - cutlass.Int32(window_left) - n_idx[0]
544	        hi_excl = center + cutlass.Int32(window_right) - n_idx[0] + cutlass.Int32(1)
545	        m_below = max(cutlass.Int32(32) - hi_excl, cutlass.Int32(0))
546	        below = utils.shr_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(m_below))
547	        n_above = max(lo, cutlass.Int32(0))
548	        above = utils.shl_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(n_above))
549	        result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
550	        result[0] = below & above
551	        return result.load()
552	
553	    return _cute_sliding_window_mask_vec
554	
555	
556	@cute.jit
557	def cute_block_diagonal_mask_vec(
558	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
559	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
560	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
561	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
562	    seqlen_info,
563	    aux_tensors,
564	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
565	    block_size = cutlass.Int32(128)
566	    block_m = m_idx[0] // block_size
567	    lo = block_m * block_size - n_idx[0]
568	    hi = (block_m + cutlass.Int32(1)) * block_size - n_idx[0]
569	    m_below = max(cutlass.Int32(32) - hi, cutlass.Int32(0))
570	    below = utils.shr_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(m_below))
571	    n_above = max(lo, cutlass.Int32(0))
572	    above = utils.shl_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(n_above))
573	    result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
574	    result[0] = below & above
575	    return result.load()
576	
577	
578	@cute.jit
579	def cute_prefix_lm_mask_vec(
580	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
581	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
582	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
583	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
584	    seqlen_info,
585	    aux_tensors,
586	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
587	    prefix = cutlass.Int32(512)
588	    hi_pref = prefix - n_idx[0]
589	    m_below_pref = max(cutlass.Int32(32) - hi_pref, cutlass.Int32(0))
590	    term1_below = utils.shr_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(m_below_pref))
591	    row_in_prefix_mask = (
592	        cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF) if m_idx[0] < prefix else cutlass.Uint32(0)
593	    )
594	    term1 = term1_below & row_in_prefix_mask
595	    hi_causal = m_idx[0] - n_idx[0] + cutlass.Int32(1)
596	    m_below_causal = max(cutlass.Int32(32) - hi_causal, cutlass.Int32(0))
597	    term2 = utils.shr_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(m_below_causal))
598	    result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
599	    result[0] = term1 | term2
600	    return result.load()
601	
602	
603	# =============================================================================
604	# Packed-bitmask aux tensor mod
605	# =============================================================================
606	# aux[0] is a (batch, max_seqlen_q, ceil(max_seqlen_k / 32)) Uint32 tensor where
607	# bit k of packed[b, q, c] is the mask for (b, q, c*32 + k).
608	
609	
610	@cute.jit
611	def cute_packed_mask_aux(
612	    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
613	    head: cute.TensorSSA,
614	    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
615	    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
616	    seqlen_info,
617	    aux_tensors,
618	) -> cute.TensorSSA:
619	    packed = aux_tensors[0]
620	    val = packed[batch[0], m_idx[0], n_idx[0] // cutlass.Int32(32)]
621	    shift = cutlass.Uint32(n_idx[0] % cutlass.Int32(32))
622	    bit_set = cutlass.Boolean(utils.shr_u32(val, shift) & cutlass.Uint32(1))
623	    result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(n_idx.shape, dtype=cutlass.Boolean)
624	    for j in cutlass.range_constexpr(cute.size(n_idx.shape)):
625	        result[j] = bit_set
626	    return result.load()
627	
628	
629	def get_cute_packed_mask_aux_vec(vec_size: int):
630	    """Vec packed-mask, specialized for `vec_size`. For vec_size > 32, requires
631	    `aux_tensors[0].__assumed_align__ = 16` and num_words divisible by 4."""
632	    if vec_size <= 32:
633	
634	        @cute.jit
635	        def _mod(
636	            batch: cute.TensorSSA,
637	            head: cute.TensorSSA,
638	            m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
639	            n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
640	            seqlen_info,
641	            aux_tensors,
642	        ) -> cute.TensorSSA:
643	            packed = aux_tensors[0]
644	            base = n_idx[0] // cutlass.Int32(32)
645	            val = packed[batch[0], m_idx[0], base]
646	            shift = cutlass.Uint32(n_idx[0] % cutlass.Int32(32))
647	            result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
648	            result[0] = utils.shr_u32(val, shift)
649	            return result.load()
650	    else:
651	        num_words = vec_size // 32
652	
653	        @cute.jit
654	        def _mod(
655	            batch: cute.TensorSSA,
656	            head: cute.TensorSSA,
657	            m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
658	            n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
659	            seqlen_info,
660	            aux_tensors,
661	        ) -> cute.TensorSSA:
662	            packed = aux_tensors[0]
663	            b_str, m_str, _ = packed.stride
664	            packed_aligned = cute.make_tensor(
665	                packed.iterator,
666	                cute.make_layout(
667	                    packed.shape,
668	                    stride=(
669	                        cute.assume(b_str, divby=4),
670	                        cute.assume(m_str, divby=4),
671	                        1,
672	                    ),
673	                ),
674	            )
675	            packed_row = packed_aligned[batch[0], m_idx[0], None]
676	            packed_tiled = cute.flat_divide(packed_row, (num_words,))
677	            base = n_idx[0] // cutlass.Int32(32)
678	            packed_chunk = packed_tiled[None, base // cutlass.Int32(num_words)]
679	            loaded = cute.make_rmem_tensor_like(packed_chunk)
680	            cute.autovec_copy(packed_chunk, loaded)
681	            result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(num_words, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
682	            for k in cutlass.range_constexpr(num_words):
683	                result[k] = cutlass.Uint32(loaded[k])
684	            return result.load()
685	
686	    return _mod
687	
688	
689	def make_packed_mask_aux_tensor(
690	    batch: int,
691	    seqlen_q: int,
692	    seqlen_k: int,
693	    density: float = 0.5,
694	    device="cuda",
695	    seed: int = 0,
696	):
697	    """Random Uint32 bit-packed mask. num_words is rounded up to a multiple of 4
698	    so each row is 16-byte aligned (LDG.E.128 requirement at vec_size=128)."""
699	    g = torch.Generator(device=device).manual_seed(seed)
700	    num_words = ((seqlen_k + 31) // 32 + 3) // 4 * 4
701	    bools = (
702	        torch.rand(batch, seqlen_q, num_words * 32, device=device, generator=g)
703	        < density
704	    )
705	    bools = bools.reshape(batch, seqlen_q, num_words, 32)
706	    powers = 1 << torch.arange(32, device=device, dtype=torch.int64)
707	    packed = (bools.to(torch.int64) * powers).sum(-1).to(torch.uint32)
708	    packed.__assumed_align__ = 16
709	    return packed
710	
711	
712	VEC_MASK_FACTORIES = {
713	    "causal": ("factory", get_cute_causal_mask_vec),
714	    "block_causal": ("factory", get_cute_causal_mask_vec),
715	    "sliding_window": ("factory_window", get_cute_sliding_window_mask_vec),
716	    "block_diagonal": ("static", cute_block_diagonal_mask_vec),
717	    "prefix_lm": ("static", cute_prefix_lm_mask_vec),
718	    "packed_aux": ("factory_vec_size", get_cute_packed_mask_aux_vec),
719	}
720	
721	
722	# Scalar mods for vec masks not in STATIC_MASKS / PARAMETERIZED_MASK_FACTORIES.
723	EXTRA_SCALAR_MASKS = {
724	    "packed_aux": cute_packed_mask_aux,
725	}
726	
727	
728	def get_vec_mask(
729	    mask_name, seqlen_q=None, seqlen_k=None, window_size=None, vec_size=None
730	):
731	    """Return a vectorized cute mask callable for `mask_name`, or None if there
732	    is no vec form. Caller sets `__vec_size__` on the returned callable.
733	    `vec_size` is required for masks whose body specializes on it (packed_aux)."""
734	    if mask_name not in VEC_MASK_FACTORIES:
735	        return None

737	    if kind == "static":
738	        return obj

741	            raise ValueError(f"{mask_name} vec mask requires vec_size")
742	        return obj(vec_size)

744	        (seqlen_k - seqlen_q) if (seqlen_q is not None and seqlen_k is not None) else 0
745	    )

747	        return obj(offset)
748	    if kind == "factory_window":
749	        if window_size is None:
750	            raise ValueError("sliding_window vec mask requires window_size")
751	        return obj(window_size, window_size, offset)
752	    raise ValueError(f"unknown vec mask kind: {kind}")

762	    "mini_causal": (cute_mini_causal_mask, flex_mini_causal_mask),
763	    "prefix_lm": (cute_prefix_lm_mask, flex_prefix_lm_mask),

774	    "block_causal": (get_cute_block_causal_mask, get_flex_block_causal_mask),
775	    "sliding_window": (get_cute_sliding_window_mask, get_flex_sliding_window_mask),
776	}

779	def get_mask_pair(mask_name, seqlen_q=None, seqlen_k=None, window_size=None):
780	    """Get (cute_mask, flex_mask) pair for the given mask name.
781	
782	    For static masks, seqlen info is not needed.
783	    For parameterized masks, seqlen_q and seqlen_k are required.

from typing import Callable, Optional

import random
import math

import cutlass
import cutlass.cute as cute
import torch

from flash_attn.cute import utils
from flash_attn.cute.block_sparsity import fast_sampling


# =============================================================================
# CuTe mask_mod functions (for kernel compilation)
# All use signature: (batch, head, m_idx, n_idx, seqlen_info, aux_tensors)
# =============================================================================

# =============================================================================
# mask_mod functions that don't use global indices
# =============================================================================


@fast_sampling
@cute.jit
def cute_causal_mask(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors: None,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    offset = seqlen_info.seqlen_k - seqlen_info.seqlen_q
    offset_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(offset, cutlass.Int32)
    return n_idx <= (m_idx + offset_ssa)


def get_cute_causal_mask(offset: int):
    return cute_causal_mask


def get_cute_block_causal_mask(offset: int):
    @fast_sampling
    @cute.jit
    def _cute_block_causal_mask(
        batch: cute.TensorSSA,
        head: cute.TensorSSA,
        m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
        n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
        seqlen_info,
        aux_tensors: None,
    ) -> cute.TensorSSA:
        offset_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(offset, cutlass.Int32)
        return n_idx <= (m_idx + offset_ssa)

    return _cute_block_causal_mask


def get_cute_sliding_window_mask(window_left: int, window_right: int, offset: int):
    @fast_sampling
    @cute.jit
    def _cute_sliding_window_mask(
        batch: cute.TensorSSA,
        head: cute.TensorSSA,
        m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
        n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
        seqlen_info,
        aux_tensors,
    ) -> cute.TensorSSA:
        offset = seqlen_info.seqlen_k - seqlen_info.seqlen_q
        offset_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(offset, cutlass.Int32)
        window_left_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(window_left, cutlass.Int32)
        window_right_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(window_right, cutlass.Int32)
        center = m_idx + offset_ssa
        lower = center - window_left_ssa
        upper = center + window_right_ssa
        return (n_idx >= lower) & (n_idx <= upper)

    return _cute_sliding_window_mask


@fast_sampling
@cute.jit
def cute_block_diagonal_mask(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    block_size_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(128, cutlass.Int32)
    return (m_idx // block_size_ssa) == (n_idx // block_size_ssa)


@cute.jit
def cute_mini_causal_mask(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    tile_size_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(128, cutlass.Int32)
    m_mod = m_idx % tile_size_ssa
    n_mod = n_idx % tile_size_ssa
    return m_mod >= n_mod


@fast_sampling
@cute.jit
def cute_prefix_lm_mask(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    """Prefix LM mask: first 512 tokens attend bidirectionally, rest use causal masking."""
    prefix_size_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(512, cutlass.Int32)
    both_in_prefix = (m_idx < prefix_size_ssa) & (n_idx < prefix_size_ssa)
    causal_part = m_idx >= n_idx
    return both_in_prefix | causal_part


@cute.jit
def cute_dilated_sliding_window_mask(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    """Dilated sliding window: every other position in a 256-position window."""
    window_size_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(256, cutlass.Int32)
    dilation_ssa = utils.scalar_to_ssa(2, cutlass.Int32)
    in_window = (m_idx >= n_idx) & (m_idx - n_idx < window_size_ssa)
    dilated = ((m_idx - n_idx) % dilation_ssa) == utils.scalar_to_ssa(0, cutlass.Int32)
    return in_window & dilated


@fast_sampling
@cute.jit
def cute_document_mask(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors: list,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    doc_id = aux_tensors[0]
    m_doc = utils.scalar_to_ssa(doc_id[batch[0], head[0], m_idx[0]], cutlass.Int32)
    n_doc = utils.scalar_to_ssa(doc_id[batch[0], head[0], n_idx[0]], cutlass.Int32)
    return m_doc == n_doc


@cute.jit
def cute_ima_mask(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    bias = aux_tensors[0]
    threshold = utils.scalar_to_ssa(bias[n_idx[0]], cutlass.Int32)
    return n_idx >= threshold


# =============================================================================
# mask_mod functions that use global indices (for use with variable sequence length)
# Global indices computed as: m_idx_global = m_idx + seqlen_info.offset_q
#                            n_idx_global = n_idx + seqlen_info.offset_k
# =============================================================================


@fast_sampling
@cute.jit
def cute_global_packed_doc_mask(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    """Document mask using globally-indexed packed 1D doc ID tensors.

    aux_tensors[0]: doc_ids_q (total_q,) int32 — packed doc IDs for Q tokens
    aux_tensors[1]: doc_ids_k (total_k,) int32 — packed doc IDs for K tokens
    Mask: doc_ids_q[m_global] == doc_ids_k[n_global]
    """
    doc_ids_q = aux_tensors[0]
    doc_ids_k = aux_tensors[1]

    offset_q = seqlen_info.offset_q
    m_global = m_idx + offset_q
    m_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
    m_frag.store(m_global)
    m_doc_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
    m_doc_frag[0] = doc_ids_q[m_frag[0]]

    offset_k = seqlen_info.offset_k
    n_global = n_idx + offset_k
    n_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
    n_frag.store(n_global)
    n_doc_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
    n_doc_frag[0] = doc_ids_k[n_frag[0]]

    m_doc = m_doc_frag.load()
    n_doc = n_doc_frag.load()
    return m_doc == n_doc


@fast_sampling
@cute.jit
def cute_global_ima_mask(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    """IMA-style mask using globally-indexed threshold tensor.

    aux_tensors[0]: thresholds (total_k,) int32 — per-global-kv-position threshold
    Mask: n_idx >= thresholds[n_global]  (local n_idx >= globally-indexed threshold)
    """
    thresholds = aux_tensors[0]

    offset_k = seqlen_info.offset_k
    n_global = n_idx + offset_k
    n_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
    n_frag.store(n_global)
    val_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
    val_frag[0] = thresholds[n_frag[0]]
    threshold = val_frag.load()

    return n_idx >= threshold


@fast_sampling
@cute.jit
def cute_global_causal_window_mask(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    """Causal window mask with per-token window sizes indexed globally.

    aux_tensors[0]: windows (total_q,) int32 — per-global-q-position window size
    Mask: (n_idx <= m_idx) & (m_idx - n_idx <= windows[m_global])
    """
    windows = aux_tensors[0]

    offset_q = seqlen_info.offset_q
    m_global = m_idx + offset_q
    m_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
    m_frag.store(m_global)
    win_frag = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, cutlass.Int32)
    win_frag[0] = windows[m_frag[0]]
    window = win_frag.load()

    return (n_idx <= m_idx) & ((m_idx - n_idx) <= window)


# =============================================================================
# Eager reference functions (PyTorch/Flex Attention signatures)
# =============================================================================


def get_flex_causal_mask(offset: int):
    def _flex_causal_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
        return kv_idx <= q_idx + offset

    return _flex_causal_mask


def get_flex_block_causal_mask(offset: int):
    def _flex_block_causal_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
        return kv_idx <= q_idx + offset

    return _flex_block_causal_mask


def get_flex_sliding_window_mask(window_left: int, window_right: int, offset: int):
    def _flex_sliding_window_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
        center = q_idx + offset
        lower = center - window_left
        upper = center + window_right
        return (kv_idx >= lower) & (kv_idx <= upper)

    return _flex_sliding_window_mask


def flex_block_diagonal_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
    block_size = 128
    return (q_idx // block_size) == (kv_idx // block_size)


def flex_mini_causal_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
    return (q_idx % 128) >= (kv_idx % 128)


def flex_prefix_lm_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
    """Prefix LM mask: first 512 tokens attend bidirectionally, rest use causal masking."""
    prefix_size = 512
    both_in_prefix = (q_idx < prefix_size) & (kv_idx < prefix_size)
    causal_part = q_idx >= kv_idx
    return both_in_prefix | causal_part


def flex_dilated_sliding_window_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
    """Dilated sliding window: every other position in a 256-position window."""
    window_size = 256
    dilation = 2
    in_window = (q_idx >= kv_idx) & (q_idx - kv_idx < window_size)
    dilated = ((q_idx - kv_idx) % dilation) == 0
    return in_window & dilated


def flex_document_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx, doc_id):
    return doc_id[b, h, q_idx] == doc_id[b, h, kv_idx]


def flex_ima_mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx, bias):
    return kv_idx >= bias[kv_idx]


# =============================================================================
# Flex reference factories for global-index masks (per-sequence)
# Each factory(seq_idx, sq, sk) -> mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx)
# where q_idx/kv_idx are local (0-indexed) within the sequence.
# =============================================================================


def global_packed_doc_flex_factory(doc_ids_q, doc_ids_k, cu_seqlens_q, cu_seqlens_k):
    """Factory for per-sequence flex reference of cute_global_packed_doc_mask."""

    def factory(seq_idx, sq, sk):
        q_offset = cu_seqlens_q[seq_idx].item()
        k_offset = cu_seqlens_k[seq_idx].item()

        def mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
            q_global = q_offset + q_idx
            k_global = k_offset + kv_idx
            return doc_ids_q[q_global] == doc_ids_k[k_global]

        return mask

    return factory


def global_ima_flex_factory(thresholds, cu_seqlens_k):
    """Factory for per-sequence flex reference of cute_global_ima_mask."""

    def factory(seq_idx, sq, sk):
        k_offset = cu_seqlens_k[seq_idx].item()

        def mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
            k_global = k_offset + kv_idx
            return kv_idx >= thresholds[k_global]

        return mask

    return factory


def global_causal_window_flex_factory(windows, cu_seqlens_q):
    """Factory for per-sequence flex reference of cute_global_causal_window_mask."""

    def factory(seq_idx, sq, sk):
        q_offset = cu_seqlens_q[seq_idx].item()

        def mask(b, h, q_idx, kv_idx):
            q_global = q_offset + q_idx
            window = windows[q_global]
            return (kv_idx <= q_idx) & ((q_idx - kv_idx) <= window)

        return mask

    return factory


# =============================================================================
# Utility functions
# =============================================================================


def random_doc_id_tensor(nheads, batch, seqlen_q, device="cpu"):
    """Generate synthetic document ids shared across heads."""
    doc_ids_tensor = torch.zeros(
        batch, nheads, seqlen_q, dtype=torch.int32, device=device
    )
    for b in range(batch):
        N = seqlen_q
        max_segments = max(1, math.ceil(math.sqrt(max(N // 4, 1))))
        n = random.randint(1, max_segments)
        n = min(n, N)
        cuts = sorted(random.sample(range(1, N), n - 1))
        lengths = [b - a for a, b in zip((0, *cuts), (*cuts, N))]
        base_doc_ids = torch.repeat_interleave(
            torch.arange(len(lengths), device=device, dtype=torch.int32),
            torch.tensor(lengths, device=device, dtype=torch.int32),
        )

        for h in range(nheads):
            doc_ids_tensor[b, h, :] = base_doc_ids
    return doc_ids_tensor


def make_packed_doc_ids(seqlens_q, seqlens_k, device="cuda"):
    """Generate packed 1D doc ID tensors for Q and K for varlen global-index testing.

    For each sequence, divides tokens into sqrt(len)-ish segments.
    Returns (doc_ids_q, doc_ids_k) of shape (total_q,) and (total_k,).
    """
    total_q = sum(seqlens_q)
    total_k = sum(seqlens_k)
    doc_ids_q = torch.zeros(total_q, dtype=torch.int32, device=device)
    doc_ids_k = torch.zeros(total_k, dtype=torch.int32, device=device)

    q_off = 0
    k_off = 0
    for sq, sk in zip(seqlens_q, seqlens_k):
        # Q doc IDs
        n_docs = max(1, math.ceil(math.sqrt(max(sq // 4, 1))))
        n_docs = min(n_docs, sq)
        if n_docs > 1 and sq > 1:
            cuts = sorted(random.sample(range(1, sq), min(n_docs - 1, sq - 1)))
        else:
            cuts = []
        lengths = [b - a for a, b in zip((0, *cuts), (*cuts, sq))]
        doc_ids_q[q_off : q_off + sq] = torch.repeat_interleave(
            torch.arange(len(lengths), dtype=torch.int32, device=device),
            torch.tensor(lengths, dtype=torch.int32, device=device),
        )

        # K doc IDs (same n_docs range for potential overlap)
        if n_docs > 1 and sk > 1:
            cuts_k = sorted(random.sample(range(1, sk), min(n_docs - 1, sk - 1)))
        else:
            cuts_k = []
        lengths_k = [b - a for a, b in zip((0, *cuts_k), (*cuts_k, sk))]
        doc_ids_k[k_off : k_off + sk] = torch.repeat_interleave(
            torch.arange(len(lengths_k), dtype=torch.int32, device=device),
            torch.tensor(lengths_k, dtype=torch.int32, device=device),
        )

        q_off += sq
        k_off += sk

    return doc_ids_q, doc_ids_k


def make_global_thresholds(seqlens_k, device="cuda"):
    """Generate per-global-kv-token thresholds for cute_global_ima_mask.

    For each K token at local index i in a sequence of length sk,
    threshold = random value in [0, sk//2].
    Returns thresholds of shape (total_k,).
    """
    total_k = sum(seqlens_k)
    thresholds = torch.zeros(total_k, dtype=torch.int32, device=device)
    k_off = 0
    for sk in seqlens_k:
        for i in range(sk):
            thresholds[k_off + i] = random.randint(0, max(0, sk // 2))
        k_off += sk
    return thresholds


def make_global_windows(seqlens_q, device="cuda"):
    """Generate per-global-q-token window sizes for cute_global_causal_window_mask.

    For Q token at local index i, window = random value in [0, i] (causal).
    Returns windows of shape (total_q,).
    """
    total_q = sum(seqlens_q)
    windows = torch.zeros(total_q, dtype=torch.int32, device=device)
    q_off = 0
    for sq in seqlens_q:
        for i in range(sq):
            windows[q_off + i] = random.randint(0, i)
        q_off += sq
    return windows


# =============================================================================
# Vectorized mask_mod variants (return bit-packed Uint32)
# =============================================================================
#
# Each variant receives shape-(vec_size,) Int32 SSAs and returns a shape-
# (max(1, vec_size // 32),) Uint32 TensorSSA, where bit i of element k is the
# mask for lane (k * 32 + i). Bodies assume lane i has idx[i] = idx[0] + i, so
# they compute the packed Uint32(s) in O(1) via integer/bit arithmetic on the
# chunk-base indices instead of evaluating per lane.


@cute.jit
def cute_causal_mask_vec(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors: None,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    offset = seqlen_info.seqlen_k - seqlen_info.seqlen_q
    threshold = m_idx[0] + offset - n_idx[0] + cutlass.Int32(1)
    m = max(cutlass.Int32(32) - threshold, cutlass.Int32(0))
    result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
    result[0] = utils.shr_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(m))
    return result.load()


def get_cute_causal_mask_vec(offset: int):
    return cute_causal_mask_vec


def get_cute_sliding_window_mask_vec(window_left: int, window_right: int, offset: int):
    @cute.jit
    def _cute_sliding_window_mask_vec(
        batch: cute.TensorSSA,
        head: cute.TensorSSA,
        m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
        n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
        seqlen_info,
        aux_tensors,
    ) -> cute.TensorSSA:
        runtime_offset = seqlen_info.seqlen_k - seqlen_info.seqlen_q
        center = m_idx[0] + runtime_offset
        lo = center - cutlass.Int32(window_left) - n_idx[0]
        hi_excl = center + cutlass.Int32(window_right) - n_idx[0] + cutlass.Int32(1)
        m_below = max(cutlass.Int32(32) - hi_excl, cutlass.Int32(0))
        below = utils.shr_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(m_below))
        n_above = max(lo, cutlass.Int32(0))
        above = utils.shl_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(n_above))
        result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
        result[0] = below & above
        return result.load()

    return _cute_sliding_window_mask_vec


@cute.jit
def cute_block_diagonal_mask_vec(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    block_size = cutlass.Int32(128)
    block_m = m_idx[0] // block_size
    lo = block_m * block_size - n_idx[0]
    hi = (block_m + cutlass.Int32(1)) * block_size - n_idx[0]
    m_below = max(cutlass.Int32(32) - hi, cutlass.Int32(0))
    below = utils.shr_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(m_below))
    n_above = max(lo, cutlass.Int32(0))
    above = utils.shl_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(n_above))
    result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
    result[0] = below & above
    return result.load()


@cute.jit
def cute_prefix_lm_mask_vec(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    prefix = cutlass.Int32(512)
    hi_pref = prefix - n_idx[0]
    m_below_pref = max(cutlass.Int32(32) - hi_pref, cutlass.Int32(0))
    term1_below = utils.shr_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(m_below_pref))
    row_in_prefix_mask = (
        cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF) if m_idx[0] < prefix else cutlass.Uint32(0)
    )
    term1 = term1_below & row_in_prefix_mask
    hi_causal = m_idx[0] - n_idx[0] + cutlass.Int32(1)
    m_below_causal = max(cutlass.Int32(32) - hi_causal, cutlass.Int32(0))
    term2 = utils.shr_u32(cutlass.Uint32(0xFFFFFFFF), cutlass.Uint32(m_below_causal))
    result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
    result[0] = term1 | term2
    return result.load()


# =============================================================================
# Packed-bitmask aux tensor mod
# =============================================================================
# aux[0] is a (batch, max_seqlen_q, ceil(max_seqlen_k / 32)) Uint32 tensor where
# bit k of packed[b, q, c] is the mask for (b, q, c*32 + k).


@cute.jit
def cute_packed_mask_aux(
    batch: cute.TensorSSA,
    head: cute.TensorSSA,
    m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
    seqlen_info,
    aux_tensors,
) -> cute.TensorSSA:
    packed = aux_tensors[0]
    val = packed[batch[0], m_idx[0], n_idx[0] // cutlass.Int32(32)]
    shift = cutlass.Uint32(n_idx[0] % cutlass.Int32(32))
    bit_set = cutlass.Boolean(utils.shr_u32(val, shift) & cutlass.Uint32(1))
    result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(n_idx.shape, dtype=cutlass.Boolean)
    for j in cutlass.range_constexpr(cute.size(n_idx.shape)):
        result[j] = bit_set
    return result.load()


def get_cute_packed_mask_aux_vec(vec_size: int):
    """Vec packed-mask, specialized for `vec_size`. For vec_size > 32, requires
    `aux_tensors[0].__assumed_align__ = 16` and num_words divisible by 4."""
    if vec_size <= 32:

        @cute.jit
        def _mod(
            batch: cute.TensorSSA,
            head: cute.TensorSSA,
            m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
            n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
            seqlen_info,
            aux_tensors,
        ) -> cute.TensorSSA:
            packed = aux_tensors[0]
            base = n_idx[0] // cutlass.Int32(32)
            val = packed[batch[0], m_idx[0], base]
            shift = cutlass.Uint32(n_idx[0] % cutlass.Int32(32))
            result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(1, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
            result[0] = utils.shr_u32(val, shift)
            return result.load()
    else:
        num_words = vec_size // 32

        @cute.jit
        def _mod(
            batch: cute.TensorSSA,
            head: cute.TensorSSA,
            m_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
            n_idx: cute.TensorSSA,
            seqlen_info,
            aux_tensors,
        ) -> cute.TensorSSA:
            packed = aux_tensors[0]
            b_str, m_str, _ = packed.stride
            packed_aligned = cute.make_tensor(
                packed.iterator,
                cute.make_layout(
                    packed.shape,
                    stride=(
                        cute.assume(b_str, divby=4),
                        cute.assume(m_str, divby=4),
                        1,
                    ),
                ),
            )
            packed_row = packed_aligned[batch[0], m_idx[0], None]
            packed_tiled = cute.flat_divide(packed_row, (num_words,))
            base = n_idx[0] // cutlass.Int32(32)
            packed_chunk = packed_tiled[None, base // cutlass.Int32(num_words)]
            loaded = cute.make_rmem_tensor_like(packed_chunk)
            cute.autovec_copy(packed_chunk, loaded)
            result = cute.make_rmem_tensor(num_words, dtype=cutlass.Uint32)
            for k in cutlass.range_constexpr(num_words):
                result[k] = cutlass.Uint32(loaded[k])
            return result.load()

    return _mod


def make_packed_mask_aux_tensor(
    batch: int,
    seqlen_q: int,
    seqlen_k: int,
    density: float = 0.5,
    device="cuda",
    seed: int = 0,
):
    """Random Uint32 bit-packed mask. num_words is rounded up to a multiple of 4
    so each row is 16-byte aligned (LDG.E.128 requirement at vec_size=128)."""
    g = torch.Generator(device=device).manual_seed(seed)
    num_words = ((seqlen_k + 31) // 32 + 3) // 4 * 4
    bools = (
        torch.rand(batch, seqlen_q, num_words * 32, device=device, generator=g)
        < density
    )
    bools = bools.reshape(batch, seqlen_q, num_words, 32)
    powers = 1 << torch.arange(32, device=device, dtype=torch.int64)
    packed = (bools.to(torch.int64) * powers).sum(-1).to(torch.uint32)
    packed.__assumed_align__ = 16
    return packed


VEC_MASK_FACTORIES = {
    "causal": ("factory", get_cute_causal_mask_vec),
    "block_causal": ("factory", get_cute_causal_mask_vec),
    "sliding_window": ("factory_window", get_cute_sliding_window_mask_vec),
    "block_diagonal": ("static", cute_block_diagonal_mask_vec),
    "prefix_lm": ("static", cute_prefix_lm_mask_vec),
    "packed_aux": ("factory_vec_size", get_cute_packed_mask_aux_vec),
}


# Scalar mods for vec masks not in STATIC_MASKS / PARAMETERIZED_MASK_FACTORIES.
EXTRA_SCALAR_MASKS = {
    "packed_aux": cute_packed_mask_aux,
}


def get_vec_mask(
    mask_name, seqlen_q=None, seqlen_k=None, window_size=None, vec_size=None
):
    """Return a vectorized cute mask callable for `mask_name`, or None if there
    is no vec form. Caller sets `__vec_size__` on the returned callable.
    `vec_size` is required for masks whose body specializes on it (packed_aux)."""
    if mask_name not in VEC_MASK_FACTORIES:
        return None
    kind, obj = VEC_MASK_FACTORIES[mask_name]
    if kind == "static":
        return obj
    if kind == "factory_vec_size":
        if vec_size is None:
            raise ValueError(f"{mask_name} vec mask requires vec_size")
        return obj(vec_size)
    offset = (
        (seqlen_k - seqlen_q) if (seqlen_q is not None and seqlen_k is not None) else 0
    )
    if kind == "factory":
        return obj(offset)
    if kind == "factory_window":
        if window_size is None:
            raise ValueError("sliding_window vec mask requires window_size")
        return obj(window_size, window_size, offset)
    raise ValueError(f"unknown vec mask kind: {kind}")


# =============================================================================
# Mask registry and factory functions
# =============================================================================


STATIC_MASKS = {
    "block_diagonal": (cute_block_diagonal_mask, flex_block_diagonal_mask),
    "mini_causal": (cute_mini_causal_mask, flex_mini_causal_mask),
    "prefix_lm": (cute_prefix_lm_mask, flex_prefix_lm_mask),
    "dilated_sliding_window": (
        cute_dilated_sliding_window_mask,
        flex_dilated_sliding_window_mask,
    ),
    "document": (cute_document_mask, flex_document_mask),
    "ima": (cute_ima_mask, flex_ima_mask),
}

PARAMETERIZED_MASK_FACTORIES = {
    "causal": (get_cute_causal_mask, get_flex_causal_mask),
    "block_causal": (get_cute_block_causal_mask, get_flex_block_causal_mask),
    "sliding_window": (get_cute_sliding_window_mask, get_flex_sliding_window_mask),
}


def get_mask_pair(mask_name, seqlen_q=None, seqlen_k=None, window_size=None):
    """Get (cute_mask, flex_mask) pair for the given mask name.

    For static masks, seqlen info is not needed.
    For parameterized masks, seqlen_q and seqlen_k are required.
    """
    if mask_name in STATIC_MASKS:
        return STATIC_MASKS[mask_name]

    if mask_name not in PARAMETERIZED_MASK_FACTORIES:
        raise ValueError(f"Unknown mask: {mask_name}")

    if seqlen_q is None or seqlen_k is None:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Parameterized mask '{mask_name}' requires seqlen_q and seqlen_k"
        )

    cute_factory, flex_factory = PARAMETERIZED_MASK_FACTORIES[mask_name]
    offset = seqlen_k - seqlen_q

    if mask_name == "sliding_window":
        if window_size is None:
            raise ValueError("sliding_window mask requires window_size parameter")
        cute_mask = cute_factory(window_size, window_size, offset)
        flex_mask = flex_factory(window_size, window_size, offset)
    else:
        cute_mask = cute_factory(offset)
        flex_mask = flex_factory(offset)

    return cute_mask, flex_mask


if __name__ == "__main__":
    doc_ids = random_doc_id_tensor(1, 2, 128)
    print(f"{doc_ids = }")

def _resolve_causal_local_window(causal, window_size_left, window_size_right, mask_mod=None):
    """Resolve causal/local/window settings into canonical form.

    Returns (causal, local, window_size_left, window_size_right).
    """
    if mask_mod is not None:
        return False, False, window_size_left, window_size_right
    if causal:
        window_size_right = 0
    if window_size_left is not None and window_size_right is not None and window_size_left + window_size_right < 0:
        window_size_left = None
        window_size_right = None
    if window_size_left is not None or window_size_right is not None:
        if window_size_left is None and window_size_right == 0:
            causal, local = True, False
            window_size_right = None
        else:
            causal, local = False, True
    else:
        local = False
    return causal, local, window_size_left, window_size_right

def _flash_attn_fwd(
    q: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    v: torch.Tensor,
    qv: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    cu_seqlens_q: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    cu_seqlens_k: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    seqused_q: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    seqused_k: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    max_seqlen_q: Optional[int] = None,
    max_seqlen_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_seqlen_k: Optional[int] = None,
    page_table: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    softmax_scale: Optional[float] = None,
    causal: bool = False,
    softcap: Optional[float] = None,
    window_size_left: Optional[int] = None,
    window_size_right: Optional[int] = None,
    learnable_sink: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    tile_mn: Optional[Tuple[int, int]] = None,
    mma_pv_is_rs: Optional[bool] = None,
    intra_wg_overlap: Optional[bool] = None,
    num_threads: int = 384,
    num_splits: int = 1,
    pack_gqa: Optional[bool] = None,
    _arch: Optional[int] = None,
    score_mod: Optional[Callable] = None,
    mask_mod: Optional[Callable] = None,
    block_sparse_tensors: Optional[BlockSparseTensorsTorch] = None,
    return_lse: bool = False,
    out: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    lse: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    aux_tensors: Optional[list[torch.Tensor]] = None,
    aux_scalars: Optional[tuple] = None,
    q_descale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    k_descale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    v_descale: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    gather_kv_indices: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, Optional[torch.Tensor], Optional[torch.Tensor]]:
    """Forward pass for FlashAttention.

    Args:
        ...
        score_mod: A callable that takes the attention scores and applies a modification.
        mask_mod: A callable that takes token position information and selectively masks
        block_sparse_tensors: A tuple of tensors used for block sparsity.
        return_lse: Whether to return the log softmax of the attention scores. If set to True will always calculate
            The returned LSE supports taking gradient.
        out: Optional pre-allocated output tensor. If None, will be allocated internally.
        lse: Optional pre-allocated log-sum-exp tensor. If None, will be allocated when needed.
        aux_tensors: Some score_mods will want to read from global aux_tensors. This is how we thread them through to the inner kernel.
        aux_scalars: Runtime scalar captures used by score_mod or mask_mod.
    """
    aux_scalars = tuple(aux_scalars) if aux_scalars else None
    q, k, v, qv = [maybe_contiguous(t) for t in (q, k, v, qv)]
    assert q is not None or qv is not None
    assert v is not None
    q_descale, k_descale, v_descale = [maybe_contiguous(t) for t in (q_descale, k_descale, v_descale)]
    q_shape = q.shape if q is not None else qv.shape
    num_head, head_dim = q_shape[-2:]
    if cu_seqlens_q is None:
        batch_size, seqlen_q = q_shape[:2]
        total_q = batch_size * seqlen_q
    else:
        batch_size = cu_seqlens_q.shape[0] - 1
        seqlen_q = None
        total_q = q_shape[0]
    if page_table is not None:
